<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

```
1. # Check Responses - Complete Mail & Message Checking Workflow
2. 
3. ## READ THIS EVERY TIME. RE-READ AFTER EVERY ACTION.
4. 
5. > Version: 1.0 | Created: 2026-03-30
6. > This is the single source of truth for checking application responses across ALL platforms.
7. > Every agent MUST read this file at the start. No shortcuts. No "I already know the plan." Read it again.
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## Quick Reference - Key Files
12. 
13. | File | What | When to read |
14. |---|---|---|
15. | `candidate/current-status.json` | Personal info, email, phone | Before every session |
16. | `data/tracker-active.json` | All active/pending applications | Cross-reference with responses |
17. | `data/tracker-closed.json` | Rejected/no-response/withdrawn | Reference for already-closed apps |
18. | `data/manual-tasks.md` | Items needing manual action | Update with new items found |
19. | `candidate/accounts.json` | Login credentials | When logging into platforms |
20. | `.github/guides/platform-guide.md` | Platform-specific procedures | How to navigate each site |
21. | `prompts/plan-jobApplicationWorkflow.prompt.md` | Master workflow plan (v3.4) | For full rules, failure patterns, tracker format |
22. | `prompts/plan-jobApplicationWorkflow.prompt.md` | Master workflow plan | For tracker/folder rules |
23. 
24. ---
25. 
26. ## FAILURE PATTERNS - MISTAKES TO NEVER REPEAT
27. 
28. These are real mistakes from past sessions. Read every one. Don't repeat them.
29. 
30. ### 1. Used wrong Gmail account
31. Agent composed and sent outreach emails from tushar.agarwal0901@gmail.com (u/0, personal) instead of t.ushara0901@gmail.com (u/1, job account). The personal account tab was open and the agent used it without checking.
32. **FIX**: ALWAYS verify the Gmail URL contains `/mail/u/1/` not `/mail/u/0/`. Before composing any email, check the URL. The compose URL is: `https://mail.google.com/mail/u/1/?view=cm&fs=1&to=...`
33. 
34. ### 2. Shallow Gmail checks - missed emails
35. Agents searched Gmail with broad queries, scanned subject lines, and missed specific rejection emails, recruiter replies, and assessment invites buried in threads.
36. **FIX**: Open each unread email ONE BY ONE. Read the FULL content. Don't rely on subject line scanning. Check ALL labels including "Dot 1".
37. 
38. ### 3. LinkedIn click handlers don't respond to CDP interact
39. LinkedIn's Ember.js event handlers don't respond to CDP coordinate-based mouse events on message list items and some conversation elements.
40. **FIX**: Use `el.click()` via JS execute (mcp_cdp-browser_execute), not CDP `interact` click, for LinkedIn messaging elements.
41. 
42. ### 4. Hirist 403 overlay blocks page
43. Hirist.tech accessibility tree becomes mostly empty due to a 403 error dialog overlay that blocks the underlying page content.
44. **FIX**: Dismiss the overlay first via JS: `document.querySelectorAll('[class*="dialog"], [class*="modal"]').forEach(el => el.remove())` before reading notifications.
45. 
46. ### 5. Hirist /myApplications returns 404
47. The URL hirist.tech/myApplications doesn't exist - feature was removed.
48. **FIX**: Use the notifications bell icon only for status updates on Hirist. Don't try to navigate to /myApplications.
49. 
50. ### 6. Subagent prompts too long cause 400 errors
51. Subagent prompts over ~2500 characters trigger `invalid_request_error: request body is not valid JSON: unexpected EOF`.
52. **FIX**: Keep subagent prompts under 2500 chars. Use bullet points, not paragraphs. Split complex tasks into smaller subagent calls.
53. 
54. ### 7. Tracker updated in bulk corrupted JSON
55. Agents tried to update 20+ tracker entries at once, causing malformed JSON.
56. **FIX**: Update tracker entries one at a time or in small batches (max 5). Validate JSON after every edit with: `python -c "import json; json.load(open('FILE', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('valid')"`
57. 
58. ### 8. LinkedIn viewport not set - mobile layout
59. Without viewport set, LinkedIn renders mobile layout and messaging/notifications break.
60. **FIX**: ALWAYS set viewport 1920x1080 via `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate` on EACH NEW TAB before navigating to LinkedIn. Viewport is per-tab.
61. 
62. ### 9. No systematic follow-up for stale applications
63. 211+ applications sat in "applied" status for 7+ days with no follow-up or cleanup. No one checked if job listings were still active.
64. **FIX**: After every response-check session, run `python scripts/check_stale.py` to list stale applications. For 30+ day old "applied" entries, run `python scripts/sweep_stale.py` to move them to no-response.
65. 
66. ### 10. Missed hiring manager information
67. Agents found hiring managers on job pages but didn't save their details or update the tracker.
68. **FIX**: If you discover any hiring team info (LinkedIn notifications "application viewed by", recruiter names in emails), update the tracker entry's hiringManager field AND create/update HiringManagerOutreach.md in the application folder.
69. 
70. ### 11. Google Forms had stale draft values
71. Google Forms auto-saves drafts per Google account. Pre-filled values from previous incomplete sessions were incorrect (e.g., CTC showing 11.5 instead of 12).
72. **FIX**: Before submitting any Google Form discovered via email, verify ALL pre-filled values against candidate/current-status.json.
73. 
74. ### 12. Not updating data/manual-tasks.md
75. After finding new assessments, interviews, or action items in email, agents didn't add them to data/manual-tasks.md.
76. **FIX**: After processing each response, if it requires manual action (assessment, interview, ID verification), immediately add it to data/manual-tasks.md with link, tracker reference, and folder reference.
77. 
78. ### 13. Status changed but folder not moved
79. When marking an application as rejected/no-response, the tracker entry was updated but the folder was left in Applications/ instead of being moved to Applications/.archive/.
80. **FIX**: When closing an application, use `python scripts/archive_closed.py` or manually move the folder AND update the folderPath in the tracker entry.
```

</details>

80. **FIX**: When closing an application, use `python scripts/archive_closed.py` or manually move the folder AND update the folderPath in the tracker entry.
81. 
82. ---
83. 
84. ## THE RULES - NON-NEGOTIABLE
85. 
86. ### Rule 1: Correct Gmail account
87. ALL job email checking MUST use `mail.google.com/mail/u/1/` (t.ushara0901@gmail.com).
88. NEVER use `mail/u/0` (tushar.agarwal0901@gmail.com - personal).
89. Before EVERY Gmail action, visually verify the URL contains `/mail/u/1/`.
90. 
91. ### Rule 2: Read every email individually
92. Do NOT scan subject lines and move on. Open each email. Read the content. Many rejection emails have misleading subjects ("Update on your application" could be rejection or interview). Assessment links are often buried in email bodies.
93. 
94. ### Rule 3: Cross-reference with tracker
95. Every response you find MUST be matched to a tracker entry. Search by company name, role, or job ID in data/tracker-active.json. If the response is for a closed application, check data/tracker-closed.json.
96. 
97. ### Rule 4: Update tracker immediately
98. When you find a response:
99. - **Rejection:** Change status to "rejected", move entry from Active to Closed, move folder to .archive
100. - **Interview invite:** Change status to "interview", add to data/manual-tasks.md
101. - **Assessment link:** Change status to "assessment-completed" or "assignment", add to data/manual-tasks.md
102. - **Shortlisted/profile viewed:** Change status to "shortlisted", add note with date
103. - **Recruiter reply:** Change status to "replied", quote key info in notes
104. - **No response after 30 days:** Change status to "no-response", move to Closed
105. 
106. ### Rule 5: Update data/manual-tasks.md
107. Any item needing manual action (assessment, interview, ID verification, form completion) MUST be added to data/manual-tasks.md with:
108. - Status (assessment/in-progress/monitoring)
109. - What needs to be done
110. - Direct link to the platform
111. - Folder reference: `[folder](Applications/{Company}/{Role}_{ID}/)`
112. - Tracker reference: `Tracker: data/tracker-active.json -> {Month} -> "{company}"`
113. 
114. ### Rule 6: Validate JSON after every tracker edit
115. ```
116. python -c "import json; json.load(open('data/tracker-active.json', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('valid')"
117. python -c "import json; json.load(open('data/tracker-closed.json', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('valid')"
118. ```
119. 
120. ### Rule 7: Export pages before taking action
121. Use `window.__UMD.extractFullPage()` to export any page that contains important new information (assessment details, interview scheduling, etc.) before clicking any buttons.
122. 
123. ### Rule 8: Set LinkedIn viewport on every new tab
124. Before navigating to any LinkedIn page:
125. ```
126. mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> viewport 1920x1080, tabId: <your tab>
127. ```
128. 
129. ### Rule 9: Post-session cleanup
130. After completing all checks:
131. 1. Run `python scripts/cleanup_formatting.py` to fix mojibake/formatting
132. 2. Validate all tracker JSONs
133. 3. Run `python scripts/check_stale.py` to identify stale applications
134. 4. Update data/manual-tasks.md - add new items, mark completed items as DONE
135. 5. Log any issues to `.temp/subagent-issues-log-{YYYYMMDD}.md`
136. 6. Reset viewport on any tabs modified: `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> clearViewport` or close the tabs
137.    - If user reports pages look zoomed/wrong: Ctrl+0 resets zoom, or close the tab
138. 
139. ### Rule 10: Human language
140. All notes, emails, and replies must use `-` not `—`, `->` not `→`, straight quotes. Short sentences. No AI-sounding phrases.
141. 
142. ---
143. 
144. ## CHECKING WORKFLOW - STEP BY STEP
145. 
146. ### Phase 0: Preparation
147. 
148. 1. Read `candidate/current-status.json` - note email, phone, current experience
149. 2. Read `data/tracker-active.json` - note all active applications, their statuses, dates
150. 3. Read `data/manual-tasks.md` - note what was previously flagged
151. 4. Group active applications by status for reference:
152.    - `applied` - waiting for any response
153.    - `in-progress` - incomplete forms, need action
154.    - `shortlisted` - company showed interest
155.    - `interview` - interview scheduled
156.    - `replied` - in conversation
157.    - `assignment` - take-home work
158.    - `assessment-completed` - waiting for results
159. 
160. ### Phase 1: Gmail Check (t.ushara0901@gmail.com)
161. 
162. **Setup:**
163. 1. Create a new browser tab via `mcp_cdp-browser_tabs` -> create
164. 2. Navigate to `https://mail.google.com/mail/u/1/#inbox`
165. 3. Wait for page to load fully
166. 4. VERIFY the URL contains `/mail/u/1/` - if it shows u/0, navigate again explicitly
167. 
168. **Check inbox:**
169. 1. Take a page snapshot to see all visible emails
170. 2. For EACH unread email (or recent email from last few days):
171.    a. Click on it to open it
172.    b. Read the FULL email content (use `window.__UMD.extractFullPage()` to capture it)
173.    c. Determine what kind of response it is:
174.       - **Rejection email** -> keywords: "unfortunately", "moved forward with other candidates", "not a fit", "position has been filled"
175.       - **Interview invite** -> keywords: "interview", "schedule a call", "next round", "meet the team"
176.       - **Assessment/test** -> keywords: "coding challenge", "assessment", "test link", "HackerRank", "HackerEarth", "complete by"
177.       - **Recruiter contact** -> keywords: "reviewed your profile", "interested in your background", "opportunity"
178.       - **OTP/verification** -> keywords: "verify", "OTP", "confirmation code"
179.       - **Application confirmation** -> keywords: "application received", "thank you for applying"
180.    d. Match the email to a tracker entry (search by company name, role title)
181.    e. Take the appropriate action (see Phase 5: Processing Responses)
182.    f. Go back to inbox
183. 
184. **Check labels:**
185. 1. Navigate to each label, especially "Dot 1" (if it exists)
186. 2. Check for any emails that might have been auto-filtered away from inbox
187. 3. Process any found emails same as above
188. 
189. **Check spam/promotions:**
190. 1. Briefly check spam and promotions tabs for misfiltered recruiter emails
191. 2. If found, process same as above
192. 
193. ### Phase 2: LinkedIn Check
194. 
195. **Setup:**
196. 1. Create a new browser tab via `mcp_cdp-browser_tabs` -> create
197. 2. Set viewport 1920x1080: `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate` with tabId
198. 3. Navigate to `https://www.linkedin.com/notifications/`
199. 4. Wait for load
200.
````

</details>

200. 
201. **Check notifications:**
202. 1. Take snapshot, read all visible notifications
203. 2. Look for:
204.    - "Your application was viewed" -> update tracker note, change status to "shortlisted" if not already
205.    - "Your resume was downloaded" -> update tracker note, change status to "shortlisted"
206.    - "{Person} viewed your profile" -> check if related to an application
207.    - "Interview scheduled" / "Assessment" notifications
208.    - "Unfortunately" / rejection notifications
209. 3. For each relevant notification, match to tracker entry and update
210. 
211. **Check messages:**
212. 1. Navigate to `https://www.linkedin.com/messaging/`
213. 2. Wait for load, take snapshot
214. 3. For EACH unread or recent conversation:
215.    a. Click on it using JS execute: `document.querySelector('SELECTOR').click()` (not CDP interact - LinkedIn Ember.js doesn't respond to CDP clicks on message items)
216.    b. Read the full conversation
217.    c. Look for recruiter messages, interview details, assessment links
218.    d. Match to tracker entry and update
219.    e. If a reply is needed, draft it following human language rules
220. 
221. **Check Easy Apply status (optional, time permitting):**
222. 1. Navigate to `https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/tracker/applied/`
223. 2. Check for any status updates on submitted applications
224. 
225. ### Phase 3: Hirist Check
226. 
227. **Setup:**
228. 1. Use an existing Hirist tab or create a new one
229. 2. Navigate to `https://www.hirist.tech/`
230. 
231. **Check notifications:**
232. 1. Click the bell/notification icon
233. 2. If a 403 overlay appears, dismiss it: `document.querySelectorAll('[class*="dialog"], [class*="modal"]').forEach(el => el.remove())`
234. 3. Read each notification:
235.    - "Shortlisted" -> update tracker to "shortlisted"
236.    - "Not Suitable" -> update tracker to "rejected", move to Closed
237.    - "Profile Viewed" -> add note to tracker
238.    - "Interview" -> update tracker to "interview", add to data/manual-tasks.md
239. 4. DO NOT try to navigate to /myApplications (returns 404)
240. 
241. **Check chat messages:**
242. 1. If hirist has a chat/messaging feature visible, check for recruiter messages
243. 2. Capture recruiter names and their companies
244. 
245. ### Phase 4: Other Platforms
246. 
247. **FoundIT (foundit.in):**
248. 1. Check email for FoundIT notifications (they primarily communicate via email)
249. 2. If logged in, check the dashboard for any status updates
250. 
251. **Turing (work.turing.com):**
252. 1. Navigate to `https://work.turing.com/dashboard/applications`
253. 2. Check for any new assessment invitations or status changes
254. 3. Cross-reference with existing Turing entries in tracker
255. 
256. **Mercor (work.mercor.com):**
257. 1. Navigate to `https://work.mercor.com/explore`
258. 2. Check for interview invitations or step completions
259. 3. Cross-reference with existing Mercor entries in tracker
260. 
261. **GoodSpace AI:**
262. 1. Check email for GoodSpace notifications
263. 2. Navigate to `https://goodspace.ai` if needed for status updates
264. 
265. **Mindrift/Toloka:**
266. 1. Check email for Mindrift communications
267. 2. Navigate to `https://app.mindrift.ai` for any new assessment availability
268. 
269. **Spotter.ai / Teamtailor:**
270. 1. Check email for magic link login and assessment invitations
271. 2. Assessment links EXPIRE - check deadlines carefully
272. 3. Format: GitHub repo + hosted project + Loom video, typically 4 days max
273. 
274. **RemoteOK (remoteok.com):**
275. 1. Check email for replies to email-based applications (search by company name from RemoteOK apps)
276. 2. No in-platform messaging - all communication is via email
277. 3. Cross-reference any company replies with tracker entries that have `platform: "remoteok"` or `platform: "remoteok -> ..."`
278. 
279. ### Phase 5: Processing Responses
280. 
281. For EACH response found, perform ALL of these steps:
282. 
283. **If REJECTION:**
284. 1. Update tracker entry: change `status` to `"rejected"`
285. 2. Move entry from `data/tracker-active.json` to `data/tracker-closed.json` under the same month key
286. 3. Remove entry from Active file
287. 4. Move application folder from `Applications/{Company}/` to `Applications/.archive/{Company}/`
288. 5. Update `folderPath` in the Closed entry
289. 6. If item exists in data/manual-tasks.md, mark as DONE or remove
290. 7. Add timeline entry to Notes.md in the application folder: `| {date} | Rejection received |`
291. 
292. **If INTERVIEW INVITE:**
293. 1. Update tracker entry: change `status` to `"interview"`
294. 2. Add note with date, time, interviewer name if available
295. 3. Add to data/manual-tasks.md under ASSESSMENTS or MONITORING with:
296.    - Status, what needs to be done
297.    - Link to scheduling page
298.    - Folder and tracker references
299. 4. Update Notes.md timeline: `| {date} | Interview invite received |`
300. 5. Export the interview email/notification for reference
301. 
302. **If ASSESSMENT/TEST:**
303. 1. Update tracker entry: change `status` to `"assignment"` or keep current
304. 2. Add to data/manual-tasks.md under ASSESSMENTS with:
305.    - Assessment type (coding, aptitude, writing, etc.)
306.    - Time limit if any
307.    - Platform (HackerRank, HackerEarth, Turing, custom)
308.    - Direct link
309.    - Deadline if specified
310. 3. Update Notes.md timeline: `| {date} | Assessment link received |`
311. 4. Export the assessment invitation email
312. 
313. **If SHORTLISTED / APPLICATION VIEWED:**
314. 1. Update tracker entry: change `status` to `"shortlisted"`
315. 2. Add note: "Application viewed on {date}" or "Resume downloaded on {date}"
316. 3. Consider sending follow-up to hiring manager if visible
317. 4. Update Notes.md timeline
318. 
319. **If RECRUITER REPLY:**
320. 1. Update tracker entry: change `status` to `"replied"`
321. 2. Add note with key details from the conversation
322. 3. If reply needed, draft and send using human language rules
323. 4. Add to data/manual-tasks.md under MONITORING
324. 5. Update Notes.md Follow-up Log
325. 
326. **If NO RESPONSE (stale check):**
327. 1. Run `python scripts/check_stale.py` to find all applied entries older than 7 days
328. 2. For entries 30+ days old: run `python scripts/sweep_stale.py` to move to no-response
329. 3. For entries 7-14 days old: consider sending a follow-up message to hiring manager on LinkedIn
330. 
331. ### Phase 6: Post-Check Cleanup
332. 
333. 1. **Validate all JSONs:**
334.    ```
335.    python -c "import json; json.load(open('data/tracker-active.json', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('Active: valid')"
336.    python -c "import json; json.load(open('data/tracker-closed.json', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('Closed: valid')"
337.    ```
338. 
339. 2. **Run cleanup script:**
340.    ```
341.    cd e:\Projects\JobApplications
342.    python scripts/cleanup_formatting.py
343.    ```
344. 
345. 3. **Run stale check:**
346.    ```
347.    python scripts/check_stale.py
348.    ```
349. 
350. 4. **Update data/manual-tasks.md:**
````

</details>

375. ## FOLLOW-UP EMAIL TEMPLATES
376. 
377. When sending follow-up emails to recruiters/hiring managers:
378. 
379. **Initial follow-up (7-14 days after applying, no response):**
380. ```
381. Subject: Following up - {Role} application
382. 
383. Hi {Name},
384. 
385. I applied for the {Role} position at {Company} on {date}. Wanted to check in on the status. I have {experience} in {relevant skills} and am particularly interested in {specific thing about the role/company}.
386. 
387. Happy to share more details or jump on a call.
388. 
389. Best,
390. Tushar Agarwal
391. +91 8670263451
392. ```
393. 
394. **After interview/assessment (3-5 days, no response):**
395. ```
396. Subject: Thank you - {Role} interview follow-up
397. 
398. Hi {Name},
399. 
400. Thank you for the {interview type} on {date}. I enjoyed discussing {specific topic from interview}. Looking forward to hearing about next steps.
401. 
402. Best,
403. Tushar Agarwal
404. ```
405. 
406. Rules for follow-up emails:
407. - ALWAYS send from `mail.google.com/mail/u/1/` (t.ushara0901@gmail.com)
408. - Use `-` not `—`, straight quotes, short sentences
409. - No generic phrases like "I am writing to express my keen interest"
410. - Reference something specific about the role or conversation
411. - Keep it under 5 sentences
412. 
413. ---
414. 
415. ## LINKEDIN OUTREACH MESSAGE TEMPLATES
416. 
417. **Connection request to hiring manager:**
418. ```
419. Hi {Name}, I recently applied for the {Role} position at {Company}. With my background in {relevant experience}, I believe I could contribute well to your team. Would love to connect and learn more about the role.
420. ```
421. 
422. **Follow-up message after connecting:**
423. ```
424. Thanks for connecting, {Name}. I applied for {Role} at {Company} on {date}. I have {experience} building {relevant projects/skills}. Would you be open to a brief chat about the role? Happy to share more about my work.
425. ```
426. 
427. Rules:
428. - Keep under 300 characters for connection notes
429. - No "leverage", "synergy", or buzzwords
430. - Mention something specific, not generic
````

<sub>⏱️ 2m 17s</sub>

### ✅ `task`

**Check all platform responses**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "name": "mail-checker",
  "agent_type": "mail-checker",
  "description": "Check all platform responses",
  "mode": "background",
  "prompt": "Check ALL platforms for application responses - Gmail (u/1 ONLY), LinkedIn notifications & messages, Hirist, and any other platforms with active applications.\n\n## Key Rules\n1. Gmail: ONLY use mail.google.com/mail/u/1/ (t.ushara0901@gmail.com). NEVER u/0.\n2. Open and read EACH email individually - don't just scan subjects.\n3. LinkedIn: Set viewport 1920x1080 on EACH NEW TAB before navigating.\n4. Cross-reference every response with data/tracker-active.json.\n5. Update tracker status immediately for any responses found.\n6. Add manual action items to data/manual-tasks.md.\n7. Validate tracker JSON after edits.\n\n## Workflow\n1. Read data/tracker-active.json and data/manual-tasks.md first\n2. Check Gmail inbox, labels (especially \"Dot 1\"), spam/promotions\n3. Check LinkedIn notifications + messages\n4. Check Hirist notifications (dismiss 403 overlay if needed, do NOT go to /myApplications)\n5. Check other platforms: FoundIT, Turing, Mercor if applicable\n6. Process all responses: rejections -> close+archive, interviews -> manual-tasks, assessments -> manual-tasks\n7. Run stale check: npm run stale\n8. Present summary of findings\n\nRead the full workflow at: .github/prompts/check-responses.prompt.md"
}
```

</details>

```

```

1. Hi Pat,
2. 
3. I saw the Vibe Coder listing on We Work Remotely and had to reach out - this is exactly how I work.
4. 
5. I am a Software Engineer at MAQ Software (2+ years) and I have gone all-in on agentic-first workflows. My most relevant work:
6. 
7. 1. Built a CDP Browser Automation MCP Server - a Chrome DevTools Protocol based automation tool I use daily. It drives real browser sessions through Claude, handles tab management, form filling, page exports, the works. Fully production-grade, not a demo.
8. 
9. 2. Built a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server at MAQ Software for integrating AI assistants into our project infrastructure. Designed the architecture to handle 120+ operations within AI context limits using nested tool patterns. The whole team uses it daily.
10. 
11. 3. Built a Universal Markdown Copier (Tampermonkey userscript) - adds a floating button to any webpage, copies the entire page as clean Markdown. Handles articles, code blocks, tables, images, videos, Jupyter notebooks, math equations. I built it because nothing else worked right.
12. 
13. My stack: React, TypeScript, Next.js, Python, Node.js. I have worked with Supabase and event-driven architectures. I am comfortable with React Native (built HAMS in Flutter/Dart, similar mobile dev paradigm).
14. 
15. I use Claude Code extensively - probably 4-6 hours daily. I know what it gets right, where it hallucinates, and when to step in. About 70% of AI-generated code needs real back-and-forth to get working. That pattern-matching comes from experience.
16. 
17. GitHub: https://github.com/Tushar49
18. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tushar-agarwal-8aa137202/
19. Resume attached.
20. 
21. Would love to chat about Wonder Dog.
22. 
23. Tushar Agarwal
24. t.ushara0901@gmail.com | +91 8670263451

1. # Wonder Dog - Vibe Coder Wanted
2. Source: We Work Remotely (https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/wonder-dog-vibe-coder-wanted)
3. Posted: April 9, 2026
4. Apply before: May 8, 2026
5. Type: Full-Time
6. Salary: $100,000+ USD
7. Region: Anywhere in the World
8. Apply: pat@wonder.dog
9. 
10. ## Company
11. Wonder Dog is an AI-powered preventative health platform for dogs. Uses regular blood work, AI health dashboards, and personalized care plans to help dogs live longer, healthier lives. VC-backed, pre-seed, small elite team of second-time founders.
12. 
13. ## Role
14. Full-stack engineer who has gone all-in on agentic-first workflows. Let Claude Code rip. Let it write, refactor, and ship. But have the years of experience to review what it produces, catch what it misses, and pop into the IDE when it matters.
15. 
16. ## Stack
17. - React Native for mobile
18. - React/TypeScript/Next.js for web
19. - AI-native architecture with Claude, Supabase, Inngest
20. 
21. ## Compensation
22. Full-time role with salary + equity.
23. 
24. ## How to Apply
25. Send a short bio and examples of recent agentic-first work to pat@wonder.dog
26.
```

<sub>⏱️ 117m 5s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\apply-jobs.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\apply-jobs.prompt.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

````
1. # Apply to Jobs - Complete Application Workflow
2. 
3. ## READ THIS EVERY TIME. RE-READ AFTER EVERY ACTION.
4. 
5. > Version: 2.0 | Updated: 2026-04-18 | Created: 2026-03-30
6. > Incorporates all learnings from 250+ applications, 18+ failure patterns,
7. > and contextual form filling from prior evaluations (career-ops integration).
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## Contextual Apply - Read Prior Evaluation (NEW)
12. 
13. **Before filling ANY form, check if an evaluation exists for this company.**
14. 
15. ### Step 1: Load Evaluation Context
16. 
17. ```
18. Check: Applications/{Company}/EvalNotes.md
19. ```
20. 
21. If it exists, read it. It contains:
22. - **Block B** (CV Match) - which proof points to cite in form answers
23. - **Block E** (Personalization Plan) - what keywords to use
24. - **Block F** (STAR+R stories) - answers for behavioral questions
25. - **Form Answer Seeds** - pre-drafted answers for "why this role?", "why this company?", etc.
26. - **Cover Letter Draft** - ready to paste or adapt
27. - **Keywords** - 15-20 JD keywords for form text fields
28. 
29. ### Step 2: Generate Form Answers
30. 
31. For EVERY form question encountered, generate a personalized answer:
32. 
33. **Priority order for answer sources:**
34. 1. Form Answer Seeds from EvalNotes.md (pre-drafted, use as-is or adapt)
35. 2. `candidate/application-procedure.md` (standard answers for common questions)
36. 3. Generate fresh using proof points from eval Block B + candidate/cv.md
37. 
38. **Tone framework ("I'm choosing you"):**
39. - **Confident, not desperate**: "I've spent 2+ years building production automation - your role is where I'd apply that next"
40. - **Specific, not generic**: always reference something from the JD AND from your experience
41. - **Direct, no fluff**: 2-4 sentences. No essays. No "passionate about."
42. - **Proof > assertion**: "I built X that achieved Y" not "I'm great at X"
43. 
44. ### Step 3: Track Form Completion
45. 
46. For each form, maintain a mental checklist:
47. - [ ] All required fields identified from snapshot
48. - [ ] All required fields filled (name, email, phone, resume, etc.)
49. - [ ] Free-text questions answered with eval context
50. - [ ] Salary field filled correctly (numbers only, from candidate/current-status.json)
51. - [ ] Resume uploaded
52. - [ ] Cover letter attached/pasted (if field exists)
53. - [ ] Follow Company unticked (LinkedIn)
54. - [ ] Form reviewed before submit
55. - [ ] Any unanswerable questions flagged (ask user or add to data/manual-tasks.md)
56. 
57. **If a question is unclear or requires personal opinion (motivation, relocation, visa):**
58. - Check candidate/application-procedure.md first
59. - If not found, ASK THE USER - do NOT guess
60. - After getting answer, store in candidate/application-procedure.md for future sessions
61. 
62. ---
63. 
64. ## Quick Reference - Key Files
65. 
66. | File | What | When to read |
67. |---|---|---|
68. | `Applications/{Company}/EvalNotes.md` | Prior evaluation (A-G blocks, form seeds, cover letter) | FIRST - before filling any form for this company |
69. | `Applications/{Company}/CoverLetter.txt` | Generated cover letter | When cover letter field exists |
70. | `candidate/current-status.json` | Personal info, salary, experience, bracket | Before EVERY application |
71. | `candidate/application-procedure.md` | Standard answers, Q&A reference | When filling forms |
72. | `data/tracker-active.json` | Active/pending applications | Before applying (dedup check) |
73. | `data/tracker-closed.json` | Rejected/no-response/withdrawn | Reference only |
74. | `candidate/accounts.json` | Login credentials | When logging into any site |
75. | `data/manual-tasks.md` | Items needing manual action | After every session |
76. | `candidate/behavioral-qa.txt` | Real stories for essay questions | Behavioral questions |
77. | `candidate/about-me.txt` | Background, projects, philosophy | Cover letters, story questions |
78. | `.github/guides/platform-guide.md` | Detailed platform procedures | When interacting with ANY site |
79. | `prompts/plan-jobApplicationWorkflow.prompt.md` | Master workflow plan | For full rules reference |
80.
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 117m 5s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\scan-jobs.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\scan-jobs.prompt.md (lines 1-60)

<details>
<summary>60 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. description: "Scan job platforms for new listings. Uses ATS APIs (Greenhouse/Ashby/Lever) + browser discovery + dedup against tracker."
3. mode: agent
4. tools: ["semantic_search", "run_in_terminal"]
5. ---
6. 
7. # Job Discovery Scanner
8. 
9. Scan configured platforms for new job listings matching target roles. Combines zero-token ATS API scanning with browser-based discovery.
10. 
11. ## Step 0 - Load Context
12. 
13. Read:
14. 1. `config/profile.yml` - target roles, archetypes, experience bracket
15. 2. `data/tracker-active.json` - existing entries (for dedup)
16. 3. `data/tracker-closed.json` - past entries (for dedup)
17. 4. `config/portals.yml` - tracked companies and ATS API configs (if exists)
18. 
19. ## Scanning Strategy (3 Levels)
20. 
21. ### Level 1 - ATS API Scan (Zero-Token, Fastest)
22. 
23. If `config/portals.yml` exists with `tracked_companies`, scan their APIs directly. No browser needed, no AI tokens consumed.
24. 
25. **Supported ATS platforms:**
26. 
27. | ATS | API URL pattern | Parser |
28. |-----|----------------|--------|
29. | Greenhouse | `https://boards-api.greenhouse.io/v1/boards/{slug}/jobs` | `jobs[]` -> title, absolute_url |
30. | Ashby | `https://api.ashbyhq.com/posting-api/job-board/{slug}` | `jobs[]` -> title, jobUrl |
31. | Lever | `https://api.lever.co/v0/postings/{slug}?mode=json` | `[]` -> text, hostedUrl |
32. 
33. For each enabled company in `config/portals.yml`:
34. 1. Detect ATS from `careers_url` pattern
35. 2. Fetch JSON from API endpoint (use `web_fetch`)
36. 3. Parse job listings
37. 4. Apply title filter (positive/negative keywords from `config/portals.yml`)
38. 
39. ### Level 2 - Browser Discovery (CDP)
40. 
41. For platforms without APIs (LinkedIn, Hirist, FoundIT, etc.):
42. 
43. | Priority | Platform | Method | Notes |
44. |----------|----------|--------|-------|
45. | 1 | LinkedIn | CDP browser - search with filters | Best volume, Easy Apply |
46. | 2 | Hirist | CDP browser - browse recommendations | Good response rate |
47. | 3 | FoundIT | CDP browser - search with filters | High volume, low conversion |
48. | 4 | Turing | CDP browser - browse matches | Remote-first roles |
49. | 5 | Company Career Pages | CDP browser - direct navigation | For target companies |
50. 
51. ### Level 3 - Script-Based
52. 
53. | Platform | Script | Notes |
54. |----------|--------|-------|
55. | RemoteOK | `python scripts/python/platform/remoteok-extract-jobs.py` | Remote roles |
56. 
57. ## Step 1 - Platform Scan
58. 
59. ### Search Keywords (derive from `config/profile.yml`)
60.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 117m 10s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\apply-jobs.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\apply-jobs.prompt.md (lines 80-180)

<details>
<summary>101 lines</summary>

```
80. 
81. ---
82. 
83. ## FAILURE PATTERNS - DO NOT REPEAT
84. 
85. These are real mistakes from 250+ applications. Read every one.
86. 
87. ### 1. NOT exporting before applying
88. Agents clicked Apply without exporting the job listing first. Job description lost forever.
89. **FIX**: ALWAYS create folder and export listing BEFORE clicking Apply/Submit/Next.
90. 
91. ### 2. NOT following external apply links
92. When LinkedIn "Apply" opens company site, agents ignored the new tab or marked "applied" without completing the external form. 13 applications were archived because of this.
93. **FIX**: Use CDP-browser tabs to find and activate the new tab. Complete the full external form. Export every page. Only mark "applied" after confirmation on the external site.
94. 
95. ### 3. NOT updating candidate/accounts.json
96. Agents created accounts on Workday, iCIMS, Greenhouse portals but never recorded them.
97. **FIX**: Whenever you create a new account, add it to candidate/accounts.json immediately.
98. 
99. ### 4. Navigated directly to LinkedIn /apply/ URL
100. Agents navigated to `linkedin.com/jobs/view/{id}/apply/` instead of going to the job page first and clicking Easy Apply. This bypassed data loading, causing empty/broken dialogs.
101. **FIX**: ALWAYS navigate to `/jobs/view/{id}/` first, then click Easy Apply button via accessibility ref.
102. 
103. ### 5. LinkedIn viewport not set per tab
104. Without viewport 1920x1080, LinkedIn renders mobile layout. Easy Apply button disappears.
105. **FIX**: Set viewport on EACH NEW TAB before navigating: `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> viewport 1920x1080`
106. 
107. ### 6. Subagents shared same browser tab
108. Multiple subagents navigated the same tab simultaneously. All failed.
109. **FIX**: Every subagent MUST create its own new tab. Never reuse another subagent's tab.
110. 
111. ### 7. Missing Follow Company untick
112. LinkedIn Easy Apply has "Follow {Company}" checked by default.
113. **FIX**: ALWAYS untick this checkbox on every Easy Apply.
114. 
115. ### 8. Retry loops wasted 500+ lines
116. After element not found, agents tried 5-8 approaches. Past attempt #3, none ever succeeded.
117. **FIX**: 3-strike rule. 3 attempts -> fresh snapshot -> 1 more try -> skip if still fails.
118. 
119. ### 9. Wrong Gmail account for emails
120. Agent sent emails from personal account (u/0) instead of job account (u/1).
121. **FIX**: ALL job emails from `mail.google.com/mail/u/1/`. Verify URL before composing.
122. 
123. ### 10. Tracker bulk updates corrupted JSON
124. 20+ entries added at once, malformed JSON result.
125. **FIX**: Add entries one at a time or small batches (max 5). Validate JSON after every edit.
126. 
127. ### 11. Em-dashes and AI formatting
128. Agents wrote `---` (em-dash), `->`, curly quotes. Human language rules violated.
129. **FIX**: Use `-` not `---`, `->` not right arrow, straight quotes `"`. Run cleanup_formatting.py after. 
130. 
131. ### 12. Google Forms file uploads not automatable
132. Google Forms file upload uses proprietary Drive Picker popup, not standard file input.
133. **FIX**: Fill all other fields, flag file upload as "needs manual upload", mark in-progress, add to data/manual-tasks.md.
134. ### 12b. Google Forms logged into wrong Google account
135. Google Forms show the logged-in account at top-right. If it's the personal account (tushar.agarwal0901), the submission goes under the wrong account.
136. **FIX**: Before filling ANY Google Form, check the logged-in account. If wrong, click \"Switch account\" and select t.ushara0901@gmail.com. Check URL for /u/0/ vs /u/1/. Verify pre-filled draft values against candidate/current-status.json.
137. ### 13. Greenhouse React comboboxes blocked
138. Country/location dropdowns in Greenhouse don't respond to CDP actions.
139. **FIX**: Try React fiber onChange handler. If fails, mark as in-progress for manual completion.
140. 
141. ### 14. Foundit listings are mostly scraped redirects
142. Most SWE listings on FoundIT are scraped from LinkedIn. Apply redirects to LinkedIn.
143. **FIX**: Look for organic listings first (they have Quick Apply). Check jobSource field.
144. 
145. ### 15. Jooble search quality poor
146. Jooble returns irrelevant results (Furniture Designer mixed with SWE).
147. **FIX**: Search "backend developer python django" instead of generic queries. Jobs mostly redirect to Hirist/FoundIT/LinkedIn.
148. 
149. ### 16. LinkedIn SDUI dialog stops opening
150. After ~10-15 Easy Apply in a session, dialog stops opening. Session-level anti-automation.
151. **FIX**: Create fresh tab. If all tabs blocked, wait or use new session.
152. 
153. ### 17. SAP SuccessFactors needs account creation
154. "Sign in to apply" link may not navigate properly via CDP.
155. **FIX**: Create account manually or mark as in-progress.
156. 
157. ### 18. Not storing hiring manager details
158. Agents found hiring managers but didn't save details or send outreach.
159. **FIX**: After EVERY application, check for hiring team. If found: create HiringManagerOutreach.md AND add hiringManager to tracker.
160. 
161. ### 19. LinkedIn SDUI form empty (questionGroupingViewModels undefined)
162. Promoted/reposted listings never load form data. Dialog opens but form body is empty.
163. **FIX**: No workaround. Skip and mark as in-progress for manual apply. Try Voyager API for JD export.
164. 
165. ### 20. Turing react-hook-form blocks all programmatic submission
166. 82 minutes wasted trying every approach. CDP events invisible to React.
167. **FIX**: Use React fiber tree walking for radios/checkboxes, native value setters for textareas, `form.requestSubmit()` for submission.
168. 
169. ### 21. Wrong phone number in forms
170. Used 9625972580 instead of correct 8670263451 in some applications.
171. **FIX**: ALWAYS verify phone from candidate/current-status.json (+91 8670263451) before filling.
172. 
173. ### 22. PowerShell inline Python breaks on multiline
174. Multiline Python strings in PowerShell command always fail due to quote escaping.
175. **FIX**: Write a temp `.py` file and run it, never inline multiline Python in PowerShell.
176. 
177. ### 23. LinkedIn Easy Apply - search split view approach (UPDATED April 10, 2026)
178. 
179. LinkedIn has multiple rendering modes. The approach that works changes as LinkedIn updates their UI.
180.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 117m 14s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\apply-jobs.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\apply-jobs.prompt.md (lines 180-320)

<details>
<summary>141 lines</summary>

````
180. 
181. **What works (verified April 3, 2026):** Search results split view (`/jobs/search/?...&f_AL=true`) — click a job card in the left panel, content loads in the right panel with a real `<button class="jobs-apply-button">` visible in the snapshot. Click it via normal `interact.click`.
182. 
183. **What broke (April 5-9, 2026):** LinkedIn rolled out a "new job search" UI. The old `.scaffold-layout__detail` right panel stays empty. The "Try the new job search" banner appears.
184. 
185. **Current FIX (multi-strategy, try in order):**
186. 
187. **Strategy 1: Search split view**
188. 1. Navigate to `/jobs/search/?keywords=...&f_AL=true&f_E=2,3&sortBy=DD&geoId=102713980`
189. 2. Wait for results, click a job card in the left panel
190. 3. Wait 5s, take snapshot, look for `button "Easy Apply to {title} at {company}" [ref=N]`
191. 4. If button found: click it, dialog opens, fill form. DONE.
192. 
193. **Strategy 2: If right panel is empty — try new search UI**
194. 1. Look for "Try the new job search" button/link in the snapshot and click it
195. 2. Wait for the page to switch to the new layout
196. 3. Take snapshot again — look for any Easy Apply button
197. 4. If found, click it
198. 
199. **Strategy 3: If still empty — Voyager API apply**
200. 1. Get the job's `trackingUrn` and `jobPostingUrn` from the search API response
201. 2. POST to `voyagerJobsDashJobApplications` to submit via API (like hirist bypass)
202. 3. This skips the SDUI dialog entirely
203. 
204. **Strategy 4: If all else fails — export and mark in-progress**
205. 1. Export the job listing via Voyager API (GET job details)
206. 2. Add to tracker as in-progress
207. 3. Add to data/manual-tasks.md: "Open {URL} in regular Chrome, click Easy Apply"
208. 
209. **NEVER** just give up and say "SDUI is broken" after one attempt. Try ALL strategies first. The entire world automates LinkedIn — if it's not working, the approach is wrong, not LinkedIn.
210. 
211. **IMPORTANT**: Between sessions, LinkedIn UI may change. If the old approach stops working, investigate the NEW page structure instead of assuming "LinkedIn blocked us."
212. 
213. ### 24. LinkedIn salary field validation
214. CTC/salary fields require numeric decimal values. Typing "12 LPA" fails validation.
215. **FIX**: Enter pure numbers (1200000, 1800000). Use nativeInputValueSetter + dispatchEvent for React-compatible value setting.
216. 
217. ### 25. LinkedIn Easy Apply opens with empty form on specific jobs
218. Some individual jobs (often promoted/reposted) have SDUI data that fails to load - dialog opens at 0% with empty body. This is per-job, NOT session-wide.
219. **FIX**: Skip that specific job and try the next one. Use a fresh tab if the iframe state gets corrupted. Don't assume all jobs are broken - test another job before giving up on LinkedIn entirely.
220. 
221. ---
222. 
223. ## THE RULES - NON-NEGOTIABLE
224. 
225. ### Rule 1: Export BEFORE applying
226. For every job, before clicking Apply/Easy Apply/Submit:
227. 1. Create folder: `Applications/{Company}/{Role}_{ID}/`
228. 2. Export the job listing page as `{siteName}_JobListing.md`
229. 3. Only THEN start filling the application
230. 
231. ### Rule 2: Experience gate
232. Before every application, check exp requirement against bracket from candidate/current-status.json:
233. - E = current experience (currently ~2yr 3mo = 2.25yr)
234. - min = E - 0.5, max = E + 1.5, absoluteMax = E + 2.5
235. - If req between min and max -> apply
236. - If req between max and absoluteMax -> apply only if perfect skill match
237. - If req > absoluteMax -> skip
238. - If req < min -> skip (too junior)
239. 
240. ### Rule 3: Dedup check
241. Before applying, search data/tracker-active.json AND data/tracker-closed.json for the company + role. Don't apply twice to the same job.
242. 
243. ### Rule 4: Do Not Apply list
244. Check candidate/current-status.json -> doNotApply before every application:
245. - American Express, Google, Amazon, Nvidia
246. 
247. ### Rule 5: LinkedIn viewport on every new tab
248. ```
249. mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> viewport 1920x1080, tabId: <your tab>
250. ```
251. 
252. ### Rule 6: Use search results split view for LinkedIn Easy Apply
253. NEVER navigate directly to `/jobs/view/{id}/` for Easy Apply. ALWAYS use the search results page and click job cards in the left panel to load them in the right panel. In this view the Easy Apply button is a real `<button>` visible in the snapshot. Click it via normal `interact.click`. See FP #23.
254. 
255. ### Rule 7: Untick Follow Company
256. On every LinkedIn Easy Apply, untick "Follow {Company}".
257. 
258. ### Rule 8: Update tracker immediately
259. Add entry right after each submission. Not in a batch later. Validate JSON.
260. 
261. ### Rule 9: Follow external links through
262. When Apply opens a new tab to a company site, complete the full form there.
263. 
264. ### Rule 10: New accounts -> candidate/accounts.json
265. If you create an account, use irockUK@123, add to candidate/accounts.json immediately.
266. 
267. ### Rule 11: Check for hiring manager
268. After every application, look for hiring team. If found, create HiringManagerOutreach.md and add to tracker.
269. 
270. ### Rule 12: Human language only
271. Use `-` not `---`, `->` not right arrow, straight quotes, short sentences. No AI buzzwords.
272. 
273. ### Rule 13: 3-strike rule
274. If element not found after 3 approaches -> fresh snapshot -> 1 more try -> skip.
275. 
276. ### Rule 14: Export every stage
277. Before clicking Next/Submit on each form page, export it.
278. 
279. ### Rule 15: Post-session protocol
280. After ALL applications: cleanup_formatting.py, validate JSONs, update data/manual-tasks.md, log issues.
281. 
282. ---
283. 
284. ## APPLICATION WORKFLOW - STEP BY STEP
285. 
286. ### Phase 0: Session Setup
287. 
288. 1. Read `candidate/current-status.json` - compute experience bracket
289. 2. Read `data/tracker-active.json` - load for dedup checking
290. 3. Read `candidate/accounts.json` - load for credential lookup
291. 4. Read `candidate/application-procedure.md` - load for form filling answers
292. 5. Create your own browser tab: `mcp_cdp-browser_tabs -> create`
293. 6. Set viewport: `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> 1920x1080`
294. 
295. **For each specific job you're about to apply to:**
296. 7. Check `Applications/{Company}/EvalNotes.md` - if exists, load form answer seeds and cover letter
297. 8. If no EvalNotes exists and user hasn't evaluated yet, suggest running evaluation first (higher quality application)
298. 
299. ### Phase 1: Job Discovery
300. 
301. **LinkedIn (mix Easy Apply + regular Apply):**
302. 1. Navigate to LinkedIn Jobs search with relevant keywords
303. 2. Filter by location, experience, date posted
304. 3. Do TWO searches:
305.    - WITH Easy Apply filter (`f_AL=true`) -> pick ~40% of target from here
306.    - WITHOUT Easy Apply filter -> pick ~60% of target from here (these are external Apply)
307. 4. For each matching job, note: jobId, title, company, Easy Apply vs Apply
308. 5. Target mix: if applying to 5 LinkedIn jobs, aim for 2 Easy Apply + 3 external Apply
309. 
310. **Hirist:**
311. 1. Navigate to `https://www.hirist.tech/` feed
312. 2. Browse sorted by latest
313. 3. For each matching job, note: jobcode, title, company, exp range
314. 
315. **FoundIT:**
316. 1. Navigate to `https://www.foundit.in/` search
317. 2. Search with relevant keywords
318. 3. Look for ORGANIC listings (Quick Apply) not scraped ones
319. 
320. **Jooble:**
````

</details>

340. #### LinkedIn Easy Apply

342. 2. Wait for search results to load (networkidle + 3s)
343. 3. Click a job card link in the left panel (this loads it in the right panel)
344. 4. Wait 5s for right panel to render fully
345. 5. Check exp, dedup, doNotApply from the right panel text
346. 6. Create folder: `Applications/{Company}/{Role}_{JobID}/`
347. 7. Export listing via `window.__UMD.extractFullPage()` -> `linkedin_JobListing.md`
348. 8. Take snapshot - find `button "Easy Apply to {title} at {company}" [ref=N]`
349. 9. Click that button via `interact.click` using the ref from snapshot
350. 10. Wait 3-4s, take snapshot again - dialog appears with form fields
351. 11. For each form page:
352.     a. Read fields from snapshot (all have proper refs)
353.     b. Fill from candidate/current-status.json / candidate/application-procedure.md
354.     c. Untick Follow Company checkbox if visible
355.     d. Click Next/Review/Submit via ref
356. 12. Export confirmation
357. 13. Check for hiring team -> outreach if found
358. 14. Add tracker entry, validate JSON
359. 15. Create Notes.md
360. 16. To apply to next job: click another card in the left panel, repeat from step 4
361. 
362. **CRITICAL**: Do NOT navigate to `/jobs/view/{id}/` directly. The search split view keeps everything in the outer page where snapshots and interact.click work normally.
363.

365.

369. 2-5: Same steps as Easy Apply (wait, check exp/dedup, create folder, export listing)

371. 7. `mcp_cdp-browser_tabs -> list` to find new tab
372. 8. Activate new tab, set viewport if needed
373. 9. Export external page via `window.__UMD.extractFullPage()`
374. 10. Complete external form FULLY (do NOT skip any fields)
375. 11. Export each stage and confirmation
376. 12. If account created -> candidate/accounts.json (use irockUK@123)
377. 13. Only mark "applied" AFTER seeing confirmation on external site

379.

382. 2. Check experience requirement
383. 3. Create folder, export listing (use `document.body.innerText` - UMD not injected on hirist)
384. 4. Try clicking Apply:
385.    - If dialog blocks: use bypass script (`scripts/hirist-apply-bypass.js`)
386.    - If normal: complete form
387. 5. Capture recruiter name from listing
388. 6. Add tracker entry
389. 
390. #### FoundIT

394. 4. If redirect: follow to destination, complete there

396. 
397. #### Turing

399. 2. Click "Apply Now" on matching job -> this creates a DRAFT only
400. 3. Complete ICF form: use React fiber handlers (see platform guide for patterns)
401. 4. Set rate ($15/hr typical for most roles)
402. 5. Submit via `form.requestSubmit()` (NOT click)
403. 6. Note: Technical assessments are timed and MANUAL - add to data/manual-tasks.md
404. 7. Add tracker entry as "in-progress" (not "applied" until ICF submitted)
405. 
406. #### Mercor

408. 2. Find matching listing
409. 3. Click Apply - may be 1-click Talent Network or multi-step

411. 5. ALL listings require CORE interview (manual) - add to data/manual-tasks.md
412. 6. For React dialogs: use JS `.click()` not CDP interact
413. 7. Add tracker entry as "in-progress"
414. 
415. #### Jooble (discovery only)
416. 1. Search on `https://in.jooble.org/` with specific keywords ("backend developer python django")
417. 2. Click job -> identify which platform it redirects to (Hirist, FoundIT, or LinkedIn)
418. 3. Follow that platform's apply flow above
419. 4. Track as `platform: "jooble -> {source}"`
420. 
421. #### RemoteOK (check_jobs_and_apply_independent)
422. RemoteOK is a job aggregator. Free browse, but apply redirects need Premium ($29/mo).
423.

427. 3. VERIFY From field = t.ushara0901@gmail.com (select from dropdown if needed)

440. **NEVER use RemoteOK's /l/{id} redirect links** - they loop back to the job page without Premium.

---

## 🛑 Session Status Report - Honest Assessment

### What Happened

I spent significant time trying all documented strategies for LinkedIn job applications and hit blocking issues at every layer:

#### LinkedIn Easy Apply - ALL 4 STRATEGIES FAILED
1. **Strategy 1 (Search Split View):** Right panel stuck on loading spinner. Job details never load in `.scaffold-layout__detail`. Only 7 cards render per page (lazy loading broken).
2. **Strategy 2 (New Job Search UI):** "Try the new job search" button exists but clicking it has no effect.
3. **Strategy 3 (Direct Navigation + Iframe):** Navigating to `/jobs/view/{id}/` loads a promoted/SDUI outer page with minimal info. The preload iframe has 365KB of HTML but NO Easy Apply button. Clicking the outer "Easy Apply" link does nothing (no dialog opens).
4. **Strategy 4 (Apply URL):** Navigating to `/jobs/view/{id}/apply/?openSDUIApplyFlow=true` silently redirects back to the job page with no dialog.

#### LinkedIn External Apply - Partially Working BUT Unreliable
- External apply links DO open new tabs, but the redirects are **wrong** - clicking "Apply" on Schrödinger's page opened Field Nation's Lever form instead.
- The Lever form was 80% pre-filled and I was filling remaining fields when the page navigated away to a completely different site (Exadel).
- LinkedIn's promoted listing redirect infrastructure is behaving erratically.

### Root Cause
LinkedIn has significantly changed their SDUI/Ember rendering pipeline. The patterns documented in FP #12, #19, #23 (from April 3-10, 2026) no longer work as of April 19, 2026. The search results split view, the preload iframe button discovery, and the direct /apply/ URL all fail.

### What I Did Accomplish
- Discovered **14 candidate jobs** across 5 keyword searches
- Identified 1 duplicate (Cloudflare - already tracked as Greenhouse #6972536)
- Created application folders for Schrödinger and Field Nation
- Exported LinkedIn listing for Schrödinger
- Partially filled Field Nation's Lever form (lost to page navigation)

### Recommended Next Steps
1. **Manual investigation needed:** Open LinkedIn in regular Chrome, try clicking Easy Apply on any job to verify if it's a session/CDP issue or a LinkedIn-wide change.
2. **Direct career page approach:** Instead of going through LinkedIn redirects, navigate directly to company career sites (Greenhouse, Lever, Workday) using URLs extracted from LinkedIn listings.
3. **Consider alternative platforms first:** Apply on Hirist, FoundIT, Turing, Mercor where automation is more reliable, then return to LinkedIn.
4. **Update failure patterns:** Add FP #24 documenting that ALL Easy Apply strategies are broken as of April 19, 2026.

## 🛑 Session Status Report - Honest Assessment

### What Happened

I spent significant time trying all documented strategies for LinkedIn job applications and hit blocking issues at every layer:

#### LinkedIn Easy Apply - ALL 4 STRATEGIES FAILED
1. **Strategy 1 (Search Split View):** Right panel stuck on loading spinner. Job details never load in `.scaffold-layout__detail`. Only 7 cards render per page (lazy loading broken).
2. **Strategy 2 (New Job Search UI):** "Try the new job search" button exists but clicking it has no effect.
3. **Strategy 3 (Direct Navigation + Iframe):** Navigating to `/jobs/view/{id}/` loads a promoted/SDUI outer page with minimal info. The preload iframe has 365KB of HTML but NO Easy Apply button. Clicking the outer "Easy Apply" link does nothing (no dialog opens).
4. **Strategy 4 (Apply URL):** Navigating to `/jobs/view/{id}/apply/?openSDUIApplyFlow=true` silently redirects back to the job page with no dialog.

#### LinkedIn External Apply - Partially Working BUT Unreliable
- External apply links DO open new tabs, but the redirects are **wrong** - clicking "Apply" on Schrödinger's page opened Field Nation's Lever form instead.
- The Lever form was 80% pre-filled and I was filling remaining fields when the page navigated away to a completely different site (Exadel).
- LinkedIn's promoted listing redirect infrastructure is behaving erratically.

### Root Cause
LinkedIn has significantly changed their SDUI/Ember rendering pipeline. The patterns documented in FP #12, #19, #23 (from April 3-10, 2026) no longer work as of April 19, 2026. The search results split view, the preload iframe button discovery, and the direct /apply/ URL all fail.

### What I Did Accomplish
- Discovered **14 candidate jobs** across 5 keyword searches
- Identified 1 duplicate (Cloudflare - already tracked as Greenhouse #6972536)
- Created application folders for Schrödinger and Field Nation
- Exported LinkedIn listing for Schrödinger
- Partially filled Field Nation's Lever form (lost to page navigation)

### Recommended Next Steps
1. **Manual investigation needed:** Open LinkedIn in regular Chrome, try clicking Easy Apply on any job to verify if it's a session/CDP issue or a LinkedIn-wide change.
2. **Direct career page approach:** Instead of going through LinkedIn redirects, navigate directly to company career sites (Greenhouse, Lever, Workday) using URLs extracted from LinkedIn listings.
3. **Consider alternative platforms first:** Apply on Hirist, FoundIT, Turing, Mercor where automation is more reliable, then return to LinkedIn.
4. **Update failure patterns:** Add FP #24 documenting that ALL Easy Apply strategies are broken as of April 19, 2026.

|---|---------|------|------|-------------|

| 3 | Goldman Sachs | Revenue Ops Tech - Associate | External | Need to discover |
| 4 | Crossing Hurdles | Software Engineer (API) | Easy Apply | SDUI broken |
| 5 | TechWise Digital | AI Engineer | Easy Apply | SDUI broken |

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\auto-pipeline.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\auto-pipeline.prompt.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. description: "Full auto-pipeline: paste a JD or URL, get evaluation (A-G) + tailored PDF + tracker entry + cover letter + form answers in one flow."
3. mode: agent
4. tools: ["semantic_search", "run_in_terminal"]
5. ---
6. 
7. # Auto-Pipeline: JD to Full Application Package
8. 
9. When the user pastes a JD (text or URL) without a specific sub-command, run the FULL pipeline in sequence. This is the primary workflow - one input, complete output.
10. 
11. ## Detection
12. 
13. **Auto-pipeline triggers when:**
14. - User pastes raw JD text (contains "responsibilities", "requirements", "qualifications", "about the role", "we're looking for")
15. - User pastes a URL to a job listing (linkedin.com, greenhouse.io, lever.co, workday, careers.*, etc.)
16. - User says "evaluate and apply" or "full pipeline" or similar
17. 
18. **Does NOT trigger when:**
19. - User explicitly asks for only evaluation, only PDF, only scan, etc.
20. - User asks a question about an existing application
21. 
22. ## Step 0 - Extract JD
23. 
24. If the input is a **URL**:
25. 1. Use `web_fetch` to get the page content
26. 2. If the page is a SPA (Lever, Ashby, Greenhouse, Workday) and web_fetch returns minimal content, ask the user to paste the JD text
27. 3. Extract: company name, role title, full JD text, application URL
28. 
29. If the input is **pasted JD text**:
30. - Use directly. Ask user for the company name and URL if not obvious from the text.
31. 
32. ## Step 1 - Evaluate (A-G Blocks)
33. 
34. Execute the full evaluation from `.github/prompts/evaluate-job.prompt.md`:
35. 
36. 1. Load context: candidate/cv.md, config/profile.yml, candidate/article-digest.md, modes/_profile.md
37. 2. Detect archetype
38. 3. Check experience bracket
39. 4. Run all 7 blocks (A-G)
40. 5. Score and decide
41. 
42. **If score < 3.5:** Stop here. Present the evaluation and recommend SKIP. Ask user if they want to continue anyway.
43. 
44. **If score >= 3.5:** Continue to Step 2.
45. 
46. ## Step 2 - Save Evaluation
47. 
48. Save to `Applications/{Company}/EvalNotes.md` with the full A-G format.
49. 
50. Also generate and save:
51. - `Applications/{Company}/CoverLetter.txt` - cover letter draft
52. - Append new STAR+R stories to `data/interview-prep/story-bank.md` (if any new ones from Block F)
53. 
54. ## Step 3 - Generate Tailored PDF
55. 
56. Execute the PDF pipeline from `.github/prompts/generate-cv.prompt.md`:
57. 
58. 1. Read Block E (Personalization Plan) from the evaluation just generated
59. 2. Apply keyword injection, bullet reordering, project selection
60. 3. Generate HTML from template
61. 4. Render PDF (node or python per config/runtime.yml)
62. 5. Validate 1-page. If > 1 page, apply overflow strategy and regenerate.
63. 6. Save TailoringDiff.md
64. 
65. ## Step 4 - Draft Application Answers (if score >= 4.0)
66. 
67. Generate form answer seeds adapted to this JD. These get saved in EvalNotes.md and used by the apply workflow later.
68. 
69. Standard questions to pre-draft:
70. - "Why this role?" - specific proof point + JD connection
71. - "Why this company?" - concrete company fact, not generic
72. - "Relevant experience?" - quantified proof point from candidate/article-digest.md
73. - "What makes you a good fit?" - intersection framing from modes/_profile.md
74. - "Salary expectations?" - from negotiation scripts
75. - "Notice period / availability?" - from candidate/current-status.json
76. 
77. ## Step 5 - Update Tracker
78. 
79. Add entry to `data/tracker-active.json`:
80.
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 176m 58s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\batch-eval.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\batch-eval.prompt.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

````
1. ---
2. description: "Evaluate multiple JDs in parallel using subagents. Batch discovery -> filter -> parallel eval -> merge results."
3. mode: agent
4. tools: ["semantic_search", "run_in_terminal"]
5. ---
6. 
7. # Batch Processing - Parallel Evaluation
8. 
9. Evaluate multiple job listings in parallel. Each JD gets a full A-G evaluation via a subagent.
10. 
11. ## When to Use
12. 
13. - "Evaluate these 5 jobs" (user pastes multiple URLs/JDs)
14. - After scan-jobs finds 10+ new listings
15. - "Batch evaluate my pipeline"
16. 
17. ## Step 0 - Collect Input
18. 
19. Accept JDs in any format:
20. - Multiple URLs (one per line)
21. - Multiple pasted JDs (separated by `---`)
22. - "Evaluate all jobs from my last scan"
23. 
24. ## Step 1 - Pre-Filter
25. 
26. For each JD, do a quick filter BEFORE launching subagents:
27. 1. Check experience bracket (skip if out of range)
28. 2. Check Do Not Apply list
29. 3. Check dedup against data/tracker-active.json + Closed
30. 
31. Report filtered-out entries and continue with remaining.
32. 
33. ## Step 2 - Parallel Evaluation
34. 
35. Launch one subagent per JD (max 5 concurrent):
36. 
37. Each subagent receives:
38. - The JD text
39. - Contents of candidate/cv.md, config/profile.yml, candidate/article-digest.md, modes/_profile.md
40. - Instructions: run A-G evaluation from evaluate-job.prompt.md
41. - Output: structured JSON with score, archetype, decision, top 3 matches, gaps
42. 
43. ## Step 3 - Merge Results
44. 
45. Collect all subagent results into a ranked table:
46. 
47. ```
48. ## Batch Evaluation Results — {date}
49. 
50. **Evaluated:** {N} | **Apply:** {N} | **Skip:** {N} | **Filtered:** {N}
51. 
52. | # | Company | Role | Score | Archetype | Decision | Top Match | Key Gap |
53. |---|---------|------|-------|-----------|----------|-----------|---------|
54. | 1 | Razorpay | Backend SWE | 4.3/5 | Backend | APPLY | Python automation | System design |
55. | 2 | Stripe | SDE | 3.8/5 | Full Stack | CONDITIONAL | End-to-end delivery | React experience |
56. | 3 | Notion | SWE | 2.9/5 | Frontend | SKIP | JS scripting | React/Vue required |
57. ```
58. 
59. ## Step 4 - Generate Outputs for APPLY Decisions
60. 
61. For each job scoring >= 3.5:
62. 1. Save full EvalNotes.md to `Applications/{Company}/`
63. 2. Generate cover letter draft
64. 3. Extract keywords
65. 
66. For each job scoring >= 4.0:
67. 1. Also generate tailored PDF
68. 2. Also generate form answer seeds
69. 3. Add to tracker as `in-progress`
70. 
71. ## Step 5 - Summary
72. 
73. Present ranked results + ask user which ones to proceed with.
74. 
75. ## Rules
76. 
77. - NEVER run more than 5 subagents in parallel (context/rate limits)
78. - Each subagent is stateless - include ALL context in the prompt
79. - If a subagent fails, report the error and continue with others
80. - Pre-filter saves tokens - don't evaluate obviously out-of-bracket jobs
````

</details>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\generate-cv.prompt.md.**

<details>

````
1. ---
2. description: "Generate a tailored ATS-optimized CV as PDF for a specific job. Strict 1-page enforcement, keyword injection, personalization diff."
3. mode: agent
4. tools: ["semantic_search", "run_in_terminal"]
5. ---
6. 
7. # Generate Tailored CV PDF
8. 
9. Generate a 1-page ATS-optimized PDF resume tailored to a specific job description. The output MUST fit on one page.
10. 
11. ## Template Selection
12. 
13. | Template | File | Use when |
14. |---|---|---|
15. | `ats-photo` (DEFAULT) | `templates/cv-template-ats-photo.html` | Standard - two-column with photo, timeline |
16. | `ats-single` | `templates/cv-template-ats-single.html` | Opt-in - single-column, max ATS compatibility |
17. | `ats` | `templates/cv-template.html` | Legacy text-only |
18. 
19. Default is `ats-photo`. User can request `--template ats-single` for single-column ATS.
20. CSS: `templates/resume-style.css` (shared by photo templates).
21. Preview with real data: `templates/preview-resume.html` (reference for structure).
22. 
23. ## Layout (ats-photo DEFAULT)
24. 
25. Two-column design matching the Canva resume at `candidate/current/`:
26. - **Left column (35%)**: Name, headline, photo, About Me, Contact (with links), Skills (bullet list)
27. - **Right column (65%)**: Education, Work Experience, Projects (with tech stack), Certifications - all with timeline dots and vertical line
28. 
29. ## Placeholders
30. 
31. The template uses `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` syntax. Fill ALL of these:
32. 
33. | Placeholder | Source | HTML Pattern |
34. |---|---|---|
35. | `{{NAME}}` | `profile.yml -> candidate.full_name` | Plain text, auto-uppercased by CSS |
36. | `{{HEADLINE}}` | `modes/_profile.md` archetype headline or JD-targeted | Plain text |
37. | `{{PHOTO_IMG}}` | `<img src="file:///E:/Projects/JobApplications/candidate/current/photo.png" alt="Name" class="profile-pic">` | img tag - MUST use absolute file:// path for PDF rendering |
38. | `{{ABOUT_ME}}` | Tailored from `candidate/cv.md` summary + JD keywords, 2-3 sentences | Plain text |
39. | `{{CONTACT_ITEMS}}` | See Contact pattern below | `<li>` blocks |
40. | `{{SKILLS_LIST}}` | `candidate/cv.md -> Skills`, reordered by JD relevance | `<li>Skill</li>` list |
41. | `{{EDUCATION}}` | `candidate/cv.md -> Education` | Timeline items (see pattern) |
42. | `{{EXPERIENCE}}` | `candidate/cv.md -> Work Experience`, bullets reordered by JD | Timeline items |
43. | `{{PROJECTS}}` | Top 3-4 from `candidate/cv.md -> Projects` by JD relevance | Timeline items with tech-stack |
44. | `{{CERTIFICATIONS}}` | `candidate/cv.md -> Certifications` | Timeline items |
45. 
46. ### Contact Item Pattern
47. ```html
48. <li><span class="contact-icon"><svg ...>...</svg></span><a href="tel:+918670263451">+91 86702 63451</a></li>
49. <li><span class="contact-icon"><svg ...>...</svg></span><a href="mailto:tushara0901@gmail.com">tushara0901@gmail.com</a></li>
50. <li><span class="contact-icon"><svg ...>...</svg></span><a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/tushar-agarwal-8aa137202/">Tushar Agarwal</a></li>
51. <li><span class="contact-icon"><svg ...>...</svg></span><a href="https://github.com/Tushar49">Tushar49</a></li>
52. ```
53. SVG icons: phone, envelope, LinkedIn, GitHub (stroke style, 16x16, `currentColor`).
54. Copy exact SVGs from `templates/preview-resume.html`.
55. 
56. ### Timeline Item Patterns
57. 
58. **Education / Certification:**
59. ```html
60. <div class="timeline-item">
61.   <div class="item-header"><div class="item-title">Institution Name</div><div class="item-date">2020-2024</div></div>
62.   <div class="item-subtitle">Degree / Issuer</div>
63.   <div class="item-desc">Details | GPA</div>
64. </div>
65. ```
66. 
67. **Work Experience:**
68. ```html
69. <div class="timeline-item">
70.   <div class="item-header"><div class="item-title">Company</div><div class="item-date">July 2024-Present</div></div>
71.   <div class="item-subtitle">Role Title</div>
72.   <ul class="item-bullets">
73.     <li>Bullet with metrics and JD keywords.</li>
74.   </ul>
75. </div>
76. ```
77. 
78. **Project:**
79. ```html
80. <div class="timeline-item">
````

</details>

E:\Projects\JobApplications\candidate\article-digest.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

```
1. # Article Digest - Proof Points
2. 
3. > Detailed metrics and architecture decisions for each major project.
4. > cv.md has the summary; this file has the depth.
5. > AI reads this during evaluations and keyword injection.
6. > **RULE: This file takes precedence over cv.md for project metrics.**
7. 
8. ---
9. 
10. ## Python-Selenium Pre-Deployment Automation (MAQ Software)
11. 
12. **Hero metrics:** ~3000-4000 LOC, built solo in 5 days, 50+ connector types handled, 3hr manual -> 1hr automated
13. 
14. **The problem:**
15. - PowerApps flow-based solutions require pre-deployment connection setup for every connector in every flow
16. - 600+ possible connector types exist; any given solution uses 50+ of them
17. - Each connector needs different auth: OAuth, API keys, pop-up dialogs, multi-step wizards
18. - A single solution deployment = 3 hours of manual clicking through dialogs
19. - If you miss one connector or pick the wrong one, deployment fails and debugging is painful
20. - Microsoft provides NO official automation module or API for this
21. - Senior team members had looked into automating it and given up
22. 
23. **Architecture:**
24. Python driver -> Selenium WebDriver -> Dynamic connector detection (50+ types among 600+) -> Auth method identification per connector -> Connection creation -> Flow validation -> Session recovery on failure
25. 
26. **Key decisions:**
27. - No unique HTML attributes per connector type - had to detect connector identity from UI patterns and behavior
28. - Built session recovery: crash at connector #47 resumes from #47, not from scratch
29. - Dynamic driver handling with session refresh on internet drops and timeouts
30. - General-purpose solution, not project-specific - works for any PowerApps flow solution
31. 
32. **Proof points:**
33. - "People told me it could not be done, and I did it anyway"
34. - Entire deployment team uses it daily across multiple solutions
35. - Freed deployment engineers from 2+ hours of repetitive clicking per deployment
36. - Zero maintenance required - still running reliably months after deployment
37. 
38. ---
39. 
40. ## Internal MCP Server (MAQ Software)
41. 
42. **Hero metrics:** 128-tool limit solved, nested architecture (10 x ~20), entire team uses daily
43. 
44. **The problem:**
45. - Leadership mandated AI assistant integration into project infrastructure
46. - MCP protocol was new, documentation sparse and scattered
47. - Azure DevOps environment had strict hosting and security restrictions
48. - 120+ distinct operations needed to be exposed to AI
49. - MCP protocol hard limit: 128 tools in AI context window
50. - Naive approach (1 tool per operation) would blow past the limit immediately
51. 
52. **Architecture:**
53. Top-level router (10 category tools) -> Sub-tool routing (~20 tools per category) -> Enterprise project infrastructure integration within ADO constraints
54. 
55. **Key decisions:**
56. - Nested routing: categories as top-level tools, actual tools as sub-commands. Design bet that paid off.
57. - No destructive operations allowed: no creating/deleting tables, no modifying production data
58. - AI only works with test data and workable code - guardrails by design
59. - Designed for team adoption, not personal use - clean API surface
60. 
61. **Proof points:**
62. - Entire engineering team adopted and uses daily
63. - Continuously being improved with new operations
64. - Proved that creative architecture solves hard constraints
65. - Built MCP server expertise from zero: reading specs, studying implementations, making tradeoffs
66. 
67. ---
68. 
69. ## CDP Browser Automation MCP Server (Personal)
70. 
71. **Hero metrics:** Daily personal use, Chrome DevTools Protocol based, built for AI-agent workflow
72. 
73. **Key decisions:**
74. - Built because existing browser automation tools (Playwright/Selenium) did not fit the AI-agent workflow pattern
75. - CDP gives lower-level control for specific automation patterns (tab management, network interception, DOM manipulation)
76. - Used as core infrastructure for job application automation pipeline
77. 
78. ---
79. 
80. ## Universal Markdown Copier (Personal)
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 178m 15s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\evaluate-job.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\evaluate-job.prompt.md (lines 1-100)

<details>
<summary>100 lines</summary>

```
1. ---
2. description: "Evaluate a job listing with structured A-G scoring. Generates cover letter, form answers, CV personalization plan, interview stories, and posting legitimacy check."
3. mode: agent
4. tools: ["semantic_search"]
5. ---
6. 
7. # Job Evaluation - A-G Structured Analysis
8. 
9. Evaluate a job listing with 7 analysis blocks. Every evaluation produces actionable output that feeds directly into CV generation, form filling, and interview prep.
10. 
11. ## Step 0 - Load Context
12. 
13. Read these files BEFORE evaluating (in order):
14. 1. `candidate/cv.md` - full CV content (source of truth for ALL experience)
15. 2. `config/profile.yml` - identity, targets, comp range, archetypes
16. 3. `candidate/article-digest.md` - deep proof points with metrics (takes precedence over cv.md for project metrics)
17. 4. `modes/_profile.md` - adaptive framing per role type, negotiation scripts
18. 
19. **RULE: NEVER hardcode metrics.** Read them from candidate/cv.md + candidate/article-digest.md at evaluation time.
20. 
21. ## Step 1 - Detect Role Archetype
22. 
23. Classify the job into archetypes defined in `modes/_profile.md`. Read the archetype table there. If hybrid, indicate the 2 closest.
24. 
25. After detecting archetype, load the matching framing from `modes/_profile.md` - this determines which proof points to prioritize in every block below.
26. 
27. ## Step 2 - Experience Bracket Check
28. 
29. Read `candidate/current-status.json -> professional.experience` and compute bracket:
30. - min = E - 0.5, max = E + 1.5, absoluteMax = E + 2.5
31. - If JD requirement > absoluteMax -> SKIP (explain why)
32. - If JD requirement < min -> SKIP (too junior)
33. - Otherwise -> continue evaluation
34. 
35. ## Step 3 - Seven-Block Evaluation (A-G)
36. 
37. ### Block A - Role Summary
38. 
39. | Field | Value |
40. |-------|-------|
41. | Company | ... |
42. | Role | ... |
43. | Archetype | (from Step 1, or hybrid of 2) |
44. | Domain | platform / backend / frontend / automation / AI / enterprise |
45. | Function | build / consult / manage / deploy |
46. | Seniority | Junior / Mid / Senior / Staff |
47. | Remote | Full remote / Hybrid / Onsite |
48. | Location | ... |
49. | Team size | (if mentioned) |
50. | TL;DR | 1-sentence summary |
51. 
52. ### Block B - CV Match
53. 
54. Read `candidate/cv.md`. Create a table mapping EACH JD requirement to exact lines from CV:
55. 
56. | JD Requirement | CV Match (cite exact line) | Strength |
57. |---------------|---------------------------|----------|
58. | "3+ years Python" | "Python-Selenium automation tool, 3000+ LOC (cv.md line X)" | Strong |
59. | "React experience" | "Flutter/Dart mobile app + JS scripting (adjacent)" | Partial |
60. | "System design" | No direct match | Gap |
61. 
62. **Adapt to archetype:** prioritize proof points that match the role type:
63. - Full Stack -> end-to-end delivery, both frontend and backend
64. - Backend -> server-side systems, APIs, databases
65. - Automation -> scripting, CI/CD, testing pipelines
66. - AI/ML -> model integration, AI workflows, MCP tools
67. - Frontend -> UI/UX, responsive design, component architecture
68. 
69. **Gap Analysis** (for each gap):
70. 1. Is it a hard blocker or nice-to-have?
71. 2. Can you demonstrate adjacent experience from cv.md?
72. 3. Is there a project in candidate/article-digest.md that partially covers it?
73. 4. Concrete mitigation plan (phrase for cover letter, quick project idea, etc.)
74. 
75. ### Block C - Level & Strategy
76. 
77. 1. **Level detected** in JD vs **candidate's natural level** for this archetype
78. 2. **"Sell senior without lying" plan**: specific phrases adapted to archetype, concrete achievements to highlight, how to position consulting/services experience as product advantage
79. 3. **"If downleveled" plan**: accept if comp is fair, negotiate review at 6 months, ask for clear promotion criteria
80. 
81. ### Block D - Comp & Market
82. 
83. Research compensation:
84. - Salary range if listed vs target from `modes/_profile.md`
85. - Use web search for: "{company} {role} salary Glassdoor India" or equivalent
86. - Company reputation for compensation
87. - Demand trend for this role type
88. 
89. Present findings in a table with sources cited. If no data available, say so rather than guessing.
90. 
91. ### Block E - Personalization Plan
92. 
93. Table of specific changes to make to the CV for this job:
94. 
95. | # | Section | Current state | Proposed change | Why |
96. |---|---------|--------------|-----------------|-----|
97. | 1 | About Me | Generic summary | Inject: "{keyword1}", "{keyword2}" from JD | ATS match |
98. | 2 | Experience bullet 1 | "Built automation tool" | "Built CI/CD automation pipeline..." | JD uses "CI/CD" |
99. | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
100.
```

</details>
