/oh-my-universal ## ROLE You are an autonomous web-archival agent. Your single mission is to mirror the entire NPTEL course `noc26_de07` into a local Markdown archive, then audit your own work through six successive review passes until nothing is missing or wrong. You will not ask the user questions. You will not stop early. You will not skip content because it "looks redundant." If you encounter ambiguity, log it in `RUN_LOG.md` and continue with the most defensible choice. --- ## FIXED PARAMETERS (do not change) | Key | Value | |---|---| | Course URL | `https://onlinecourses.nptel.ac.in/noc26_de07/course` | | Browser tool | **CDP Browser MCP Server** (the `cdp-browser-*` tool family) | | Chrome profile | **`Mansha Agarwal`** — strictly. Site is already open in this profile, user is logged in. | | Conversion script | `E:\Projects\Universal_md_exporter\universal-md-copier.user.js` | | Output root | `E:\Projects\EduMails\Courses\NPTEL\noc26_de07 - \<Course Name\>\` | | Final log | `\<output root\>\RUN_LOG.md` | `\<Course Name\>` = the official course title shown at the top of the course homepage, sanitized to a safe Windows folder name (replace `\ / : * ? " \< \> |` with `-`). --- ## TOOL SETUP (do this once at start) 1. **Connect to the right profile.** ``` cdp-browser-browser action="profiles" ``` Inspect the result. Locate the entry whose profile name contains "Mansha Agarwal". If the active connection is NOT that profile: ``` cdp-browser-browser action="connect" instance="\<that profile's port or User Data path\>" ``` Then verify with `cdp-browser-browser action="active"`. Abort and write to `RUN_LOG.md` if the active profile is anything other than Mansha Agarwal — never operate on a different profile. 2. **Find the course tab (already open).** Do NOT open a new tab. Do NOT navigate to the URL on a fresh tab — that may break the auth state. ``` cdp-browser-tabs action="find" query="noc26_de07" ``` Pick the tab whose URL starts with `https://onlinecourses.nptel.ac.in/noc26_de07/`. Capture its `tabId`. Use this `tabId` for ALL subsequent calls (or open additional tabs from the SAME profile via `cdp-browser-tabs action="new"` when you need parallelism). 3. **Lock session ownership** (so other agents can't steal the tab): Pass `exclusive=true` (default) on every CDP call. Do NOT close the tab at the end. 4. **Inject the converter once per page navigation.** Read the userscript file: ``` filesystem-read_text_file path="E:\Projects\Universal_md_exporter\universal-md-copier.user.js" ``` Strip the Tampermonkey `==UserScript==` header (everything before the first `(function ()`). Keep the IIFE body. Provide a stub for `GM_setClipboard`, `GM_xmlhttpRequest`, `GM_notification`, and `unsafeWindow` so the script doesn't crash: ```js window.GM_setClipboard = (text) =\> { window.__umd_last = text; }; window.GM_notification = () =\> {}; window.unsafeWindow = window; window.GM_xmlhttpRequest = (opts) =\> { fetch(opts.url, { method: opts.method || 'GET', headers: opts.headers || {} }) .then(r =\> r.text().then(t =\> opts.onload && opts.onload({ status: r.status, responseText: t }))) .catch(e =\> opts.onerror && opts.onerror(e)); }; ``` Then inject the script via `cdp-browser-page action="inject" script="\<polyfill + userscript body\>"`. Use `inject` (not `execute`) so it runs on every new document load. Re-injection per navigation is automatic. --- ## CONVERSION CONTRACT (per page) For each captured page, produce a Markdown file with this header followed by the converted body: ```markdown --- source_url: \<full URL\> captured_at: \<ISO 8601 UTC\> nptel_course: noc26_de07 section: \<e.g. "Week 3 — Lecture 14"\> content_type: \<lecture | transcript | assignment | solution | quiz | pdf-summary | announcement | reference | meta\> captured_via: \<userscript-internal | userscript-clipboard | fallback-turndown\> --- # \<Page Title\> \<converted body\> ``` ### Conversion method (try in this order, stop at first success) 1. **Userscript internal call** — preferred. After injection, evaluate: ```js (async () =\> { // The userscript exposes its conversion via the floating button click handler. // Try to find an exported convertor; fall through if not present. if (typeof window.__umdConvert === 'function') return await window.__umdConvert(); return null; })() ``` If the userscript exposes nothing globally, skip to step 2. 2. **Click the floating button + read clipboard** — fallback. - Wait 800 ms for the button to attach (`#umd-btn`). - `cdp-browser-interact action="click" selector="#umd-btn"`. - Wait 1500 ms for the script to finish (longer for pages with iframes/transcripts). - Read the captured text from `window.__umd_last` (set by the GM_setClipboard polyfill above). 3. **Direct fallback** — only if both above fail. Use Turndown loaded via CDN: ```js const s = document.createElement('script'); s.src = 'https://unpkg.com/turndown@7/dist/turndown.js'; document.head.appendChild(s); await new Promise(r =\> s.onload = r); const td = new window.TurndownService({ headingStyle: 'atx', codeBlockStyle: 'fenced' }); return td.turndown(document.querySelector('main, #main-content, body').innerHTML); ``` Mark `captured_via: fallback-turndown` so the audit pass can re-try later. ### Auto-expansion before capture Before invoking the converter on any page: - Scroll to the bottom of the page in 5 steps with 400 ms pauses (triggers lazy loads). - Click every element matching: `button[aria-expanded="false"]`, `summary:not([open])`, `.show-transcript`, `[data-toggle="collapse"]`, `button:has-text("Transcript")`, `button:has-text("Show more")`. Wait 400 ms between clicks. - Specifically, expand any "Transcript" / "English Transcript" panel — NPTEL hides these by default. ### Failure handling per page - 3 retry attempts with exponential backoff (2s, 5s, 12s). - HTTP 429 / "Too Many Requests" → wait 60 s, then retry. - After 3 failures, append the URL + error to `RUN_LOG.md` under `## Failures` and continue. --- ## DISCOVERY ALGORITHM NPTEL course pages have a left-side syllabus tree organized into **Weeks**, each containing **Lectures**, **Assignments**, **Practice quizzes**, **Solutions**, and **Reference material**. There is also a top navigation with: `Outline`, `Announcements`, `Books`, `Forum` (skip), `Discussions` (skip), `Progress` (skip), `Certificate / Exam`, `Live Sessions` (capture if recordings exist). ### Phase A — Course-level pages (capture ONCE) Visit each of these URLs (relative to `https://onlinecourses.nptel.ac.in/noc26_de07/`) and save to `00 - Course Meta/`: - `course` → `00 - Course Outline.md` - `announcements` → `01 - Announcements.md` - `instructors` (if present) → `02 - Instructors.md` - `books` (or "Books and References") → `03 - Books and References.md` - `dates` / `exam_format` / certificate-related pages → `04 - Exam and Certificate.md` - Any "About this course" / "Intended audience" tab → `05 - About.md` ### Phase B — Build the syllabus map On the course outline page, extract the entire left-side syllabus into a JSON skeleton. Use: ``` cdp-browser-page action="snapshot" ``` Then `evaluate_script` to walk the sidebar and produce: ```json { "course_title": "...", "weeks": [ { "number": 1, "title": "Week 1 — ...", "items": [ { "kind": "lecture", "title": "...", "url": "...", "duration": "..." }, { "kind": "assignment", "title": "Week 1: Assignment", "url": "..." }, { "kind": "solution", "title": "Week 1: Solution", "url": "..." }, { "kind": "quiz", "title": "Practice Quiz", "url": "..." } ] } ] } ``` Save this as `\<output root\>\_syllabus.json`. This is your authoritative work list — every URL in here must end up as a file on disk. ### Phase C — Walk every URL For each week W and each item I in `_syllabus.json`: Folder: `Week {W:02d} - {sanitized week title}\` File: `{II:02d} - {kind label} - {sanitized item title}.md` Where `II` is the item's position within the week (1-based, zero-padded). `kind label` is `Lecture`, `Assignment`, `Solution`, `Quiz`, `Reference` (mirrors the existing `01 - Title.md` repo convention). For each lecture page, additionally: - Extract video URLs (YouTube embed src, MP4 source elements, m3u8 master playlists). Append to `Week {W:02d} - .../videos.json` in the format used by `E:\Projects\EduMails\Courses\Bulk Downloader\`: ```json { "course": "\<course title\>", "week": \<W\>, "videos": [ { "filename": "WW-II-Title.mp4", "youtubeUrl": "...", "mp4Url": "...", "hlsUrl": "...", "page_url": "..." } ] } ``` - Capture the **English transcript** as a separate file: `{II:02d} - {title} - Transcript.md` in the same week folder. If no English transcript exists, write a placeholder with `\> No English transcript available` and log it. - Do NOT download video files. The user will run `bulk-downloader.js` later. ### Phase D — Linked PDFs and resources For every page captured, scan the rendered HTML for: - `\<a href="*.pdf"\>`, `\<a href="*.ppt"\>`, `\<a href="*.pptx"\>`, `\<a href="*.zip"\>` - Direct links into `nptel.ac.in/content/...` or `onlinecourses.nptel.ac.in/...` For each non-HTML resource: 1. Download via `cdp-browser-execute action="eval"` using `fetch(url).then(r =\> r.arrayBuffer())` then base64-encode and write to `\<week folder\>/_resources/\<original filename\>` via the filesystem tool. (For PDFs \> 5 MB, instead just record the URL in `\<week folder\>/_resources/MANIFEST.md` for later manual download.) 2. For PDFs ≤ 5 MB, also produce a `\<original filename\>.summary.md` containing first-page text extracted via `pdftotext` (call `powershell` → `pdftotext.exe` if installed; else write `\> PDF binary stored; install pdftotext for text extraction`). ### Phase E — First-level external references (no recursion) For each captured page, list outbound links to external domains in `\<week folder\>/_external_links.md` as a simple bulleted list with the link text and URL. Do NOT follow them; this is just a forward index. --- ## OUTPUT STRUCTURE (final state) ``` E:\Projects\EduMails\Courses\NPTEL\noc26_de07 - \<Course Name\>\ ├── _syllabus.json ← authoritative URL inventory ├── _index.md ← top-level table of contents (auto-generated) ├── RUN_LOG.md ← timeline, failures, decisions, audit findings ├── 00 - Course Meta\ │ ├── 00 - Course Outline.md │ ├── 01 - Announcements.md │ ├── 02 - Instructors.md │ ├── 03 - Books and References.md │ ├── 04 - Exam and Certificate.md │ └── 05 - About.md ├── Week 01 - \<title\>\ │ ├── 01 - Lecture - \<title\>.md │ ├── 01 - Lecture - \<title\> - Transcript.md │ ├── 02 - Lecture - \<title\>.md │ ├── 02 - Lecture - \<title\> - Transcript.md │ ├── ... │ ├── NN - Assignment - Week 1.md │ ├── NN - Solution - Week 1.md │ ├── NN - Quiz - Practice.md │ ├── videos.json │ ├── _external_links.md │ └── _resources\ │ ├── MANIFEST.md │ ├── slides-week1.pdf │ └── slides-week1.pdf.summary.md ├── Week 02 - \<title\>\ │ └── ... └── ... ``` After Phase C, regenerate `_index.md` from `_syllabus.json` + a directory walk so the file shows captured-vs-expected status. --- ## REVIEW CYCLES (mandatory — exactly 5, then 1 reverse pass) ### Cycle structure (cycles 1–5, forward order, weeks 1 → N) For each cycle: 1. **Coverage audit.** For every URL in `_syllabus.json`, verify a corresponding file exists on disk and is non-empty (\>500 bytes for lecture pages, \>200 bytes for quizzes/assignments, \>300 bytes for transcripts). 2. **Section audit.** Open each captured `.md` and verify it has: - YAML frontmatter with all 6 required keys - At least one `# ` heading after the frontmatter - For lecture files: a body section over 500 chars - For lecture transcripts: at least 30 timestamped lines OR a body of \>1000 chars - For assignments: at least one numbered question (regex: `^\s*\d+[.)]\s+`) - For solutions: presence of an answer key marker (`Answer`, `Correct`, `Solution`, etc.) 3. **Drift detection.** For 20% of captured pages (deterministic sample: every 5th file in alphabetical order), re-fetch via CDP and re-convert; compare a normalized hash (strip whitespace + ad/timestamp lines) against the stored file. Flag mismatches. 4. **Findings report.** Append `## Cycle N Findings` to `RUN_LOG.md` listing: - Missing files (URL → expected path) - Files failing section audit (path → reason) - Drift hits (path → diff summary) 5. **Fix pass.** For every finding, re-execute the relevant Phase C/D step. Update the file on disk. After fixes, append `### Cycle N Fixes Applied` listing the actions. 6. **Early-exit guard.** If a cycle finds 0 issues AND fixed 0 things, you have reached steady-state. Skip remaining forward cycles and proceed to the reverse pass. ### Reverse pass (always runs once, even if cycles 1–5 all found issues) - Iterate `_syllabus.json` in reverse: last item of last week → first item of first week. - Re-do the full coverage + section + drift audit per item. - Additionally, re-extract video URLs and PDF links and diff against `videos.json` / `_resources/MANIFEST.md`. Anything missing = bug → fix immediately. - Append `## Reverse Pass Findings` and `### Reverse Pass Fixes` to `RUN_LOG.md`. - If the reverse pass finds zero issues, write `## VERIFIED CLEAN` at the bottom of `RUN_LOG.md` with timestamp. ### Loop-prevention rules - Each fix attempt for a given file is capped at 3 lifetime attempts across all cycles. After 3, log the file under `## Permanent Failures` and stop trying. - Drift detection ignores: whitespace runs, lines matching `(captured_at|Last updated|©.*\d{4})`, ad container text, social-share button text. --- ## RATE LIMITING & POLITENESS - Insert a 1.5–3.0 second random delay between page navigations (`cdp-browser-page action="wait" timeout=\<random\>`). - Hard stop on HTTP 429 / "rate limit": wait 60 s, then resume. - Maximum 1 concurrent page (one tab, sequential). NPTEL is not built for parallel hammering. - If the page shows a login wall at any point, STOP. Append `## SESSION LOST` to `RUN_LOG.md` with the URL and timestamp, and call `task_complete` with that summary. Do not attempt to log in. --- ## RUN_LOG.md FORMAT ```markdown # NPTEL noc26_de07 — Capture Run Log ## Run Metadata - Started: \<ISO\> - Profile: Mansha Agarwal - Course title: \<...\> - Total syllabus items: \<N\> ## Phase A — Course Meta - ✓ Course Outline (1.2 KB) - ✓ Announcements (3.4 KB) - ... ## Phase B — Syllabus Map - Weeks discovered: 12 - Lectures: 60 - Assignments: 12 - Solutions: 8 (4 not yet released) - Quizzes: 12 ## Phase C — Page Captures - Week 01: 5/5 lectures, 5/5 transcripts, 1/1 assignment ✓ - Week 02: 5/5 lectures, 4/5 transcripts (1 missing — flagged) - ... ## Phase D — Resources - PDFs downloaded: 14 - PDFs over 5 MB (URL only): 3 ## Failures - \<url\> — \<error\> (3 retries exhausted) ## Cycle 1 Findings - Missing: Week 03 / Lecture 12 transcript → /Week 03 .../03 - Lecture - X - Transcript.md - Section audit fail: Week 05 / Lecture 22 — body only 312 chars ### Cycle 1 Fixes Applied - Re-fetched Week 03 / Lecture 12 transcript (success) - Re-fetched Week 05 / Lecture 22 (success — page had loaded mid-render) ## Cycle 2 Findings ... ## Reverse Pass Findings ... ## Permanent Failures - \<none\> ## VERIFIED CLEAN 2026-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC ``` --- ## INVARIANTS — CHECK BEFORE EVERY MAJOR ACTION 1. CDP active profile name contains "Mansha Agarwal" — abort if not. 2. The active tab URL is on `onlinecourses.nptel.ac.in/noc26_de07/*` (or you are intentionally fetching a `_resources` PDF). 3. The output root directory exists and is under `E:\Projects\EduMails\Courses\NPTEL\`. 4. You are not in a fix attempt #4+ for the same file. --- ## END-OF-RUN After the reverse pass: 1. Regenerate `_index.md` reflecting final state. 2. Update `RUN_LOG.md` summary section at the top with totals (files captured, total bytes, cycles run, items in `## Permanent Failures`). 3. Print a final summary to the user containing: - Output root path - Total files written - Number of weeks / lectures / assignments / solutions captured - Permanent failures count (0 = clean) - Where to find `_index.md` and `RUN_LOG.md` 4. Call `task_complete`.

28. ### KNOWN PLATFORM ISSUES
29. - **LinkedIn Easy Apply SDUI bug (2026-05-09):** New `?openSDUIApplyFlow=true` flow throws `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'questionGroupingViewModels')` in console; dialog opens but body is empty (only "0%" + Next button, no fields). Affects promoted job postings - confirmed broken on AgileEngine (4408800334) and Hoog Store (4408826216). LinkedIn-side bug, not session-side. Retry in a few days, or use Chrome extension based auto-apply when LinkedIn rolls back the SDUI flow.
30. - **WeWorkRemotely:** All "Apply now" links route through `/job-seekers/onboarding/step_3` (auth wall). Logged-in WWR session not present in browser even though account exists in accounts.json. Need manual sign-in to extract external ATS URLs from WWR listings.
31. - **RemoteOK:** Premium-walls external apply URLs (`/l/<id>` redirects to `/premium?skip_url=...`). Workaround: Google "{company} careers {role}" to find direct ATS page.
32. 
33. ### CAPTCHA/MANUAL COMPLETION NEEDED (carried forward from prior sessions)
34. 
35. ---
36. 
37. ## SESSION LOG - 2026-05-06 (LinkedIn External Apply)
38. 
39. ### Summary
40. - Searched LinkedIn with 8 keywords, found ~100 job IDs via Voyager API

<sub>⏱️ 28714m 9s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\check-responses.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\check-responses.prompt.md (lines 1-80)

<details>
<summary>80 lines</summary>

```
1. # Check Responses - Complete Mail & Message Checking Workflow
2. 
3. ## READ THIS EVERY TIME. RE-READ AFTER EVERY ACTION.
4. 
5. > Version: 1.0 | Created: 2026-03-30
6. > This is the single source of truth for checking application responses across ALL platforms.
7. > Every agent MUST read this file at the start. No shortcuts. No "I already know the plan." Read it again.
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## Quick Reference - Key Files
12. 
13. | File | What | When to read |
14. |---|---|---|
15. | `candidate/current-status.json` | Personal info, email, phone | Before every session |
16. | `data/tracker-active.json` | All active/pending applications | Cross-reference with responses |
17. | `data/tracker-closed.json` | Rejected/no-response/withdrawn | Reference for already-closed apps |
18. | `data/manual-tasks.md` | Items needing manual action | Update with new items found |
19. | `candidate/accounts.json` | Login credentials | When logging into platforms |
20. | `.github/guides/platform-guide.md` | Platform-specific procedures | How to navigate each site |
21. | `prompts/plan-jobApplicationWorkflow.prompt.md` | Master workflow plan (v3.4) | For full rules, failure patterns, tracker format |
22. | `prompts/plan-jobApplicationWorkflow.prompt.md` | Master workflow plan | For tracker/folder rules |
23. 
24. ---
25. 
26. ## FAILURE PATTERNS - MISTAKES TO NEVER REPEAT
27. 
28. These are real mistakes from past sessions. Read every one. Don't repeat them.
29. 
30. ### 1. Used wrong Gmail account
31. Agent composed and sent outreach emails from tushar.agarwal0901@gmail.com (u/0, personal) instead of t.ushara0901@gmail.com (u/1, job account). The personal account tab was open and the agent used it without checking.
32. **FIX**: ALWAYS verify the Gmail URL contains `/mail/u/1/` not `/mail/u/0/`. Before composing any email, check the URL. The compose URL is: `https://mail.google.com/mail/u/1/?view=cm&fs=1&to=...`
33. 
34. ### 2. Shallow Gmail checks - missed emails
35. Agents searched Gmail with broad queries, scanned subject lines, and missed specific rejection emails, recruiter replies, and assessment invites buried in threads.
36. **FIX**: Open each unread email ONE BY ONE. Read the FULL content. Don't rely on subject line scanning. Check ALL labels including "Dot 1".
37. 
38. ### 3. LinkedIn click handlers don't respond to CDP interact
39. LinkedIn's Ember.js event handlers don't respond to CDP coordinate-based mouse events on message list items and some conversation elements.
40. **FIX**: Use `el.click()` via JS execute (mcp_cdp-browser_execute), not CDP `interact` click, for LinkedIn messaging elements.
41. 
42. ### 4. Hirist 403 overlay blocks page
43. Hirist.tech accessibility tree becomes mostly empty due to a 403 error dialog overlay that blocks the underlying page content.
44. **FIX**: Dismiss the overlay first via JS: `document.querySelectorAll('[class*="dialog"], [class*="modal"]').forEach(el => el.remove())` before reading notifications.
45. 
46. ### 5. Hirist /myApplications returns 404
47. The URL hirist.tech/myApplications doesn't exist - feature was removed.
48. **FIX**: Use the notifications bell icon only for status updates on Hirist. Don't try to navigate to /myApplications.
49. 
50. ### 6. Subagent prompts too long cause 400 errors
51. Subagent prompts over ~2500 characters trigger `invalid_request_error: request body is not valid JSON: unexpected EOF`.
52. **FIX**: Keep subagent prompts under 2500 chars. Use bullet points, not paragraphs. Split complex tasks into smaller subagent calls.
53. 
54. ### 7. Tracker updated in bulk corrupted JSON
55. Agents tried to update 20+ tracker entries at once, causing malformed JSON.
56. **FIX**: Update tracker entries one at a time or in small batches (max 5). Validate JSON after every edit with: `python -c "import json; json.load(open('FILE', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('valid')"`
57. 
58. ### 8. LinkedIn viewport not set - mobile layout
59. Without viewport set, LinkedIn renders mobile layout and messaging/notifications break.
60. **FIX**: ALWAYS set viewport 1920x1080 via `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate` on EACH NEW TAB before navigating to LinkedIn. Viewport is per-tab.
61. 
62. ### 9. No systematic follow-up for stale applications
63. 211+ applications sat in "applied" status for 7+ days with no follow-up or cleanup. No one checked if job listings were still active.
64. **FIX**: After every response-check session, run `python scripts/check_stale.py` to list stale applications. For 30+ day old "applied" entries, run `python scripts/sweep_stale.py` to move them to no-response.
65. 
66. ### 10. Missed hiring manager information
67. Agents found hiring managers on job pages but didn't save their details or update the tracker.
68. **FIX**: If you discover any hiring team info (LinkedIn notifications "application viewed by", recruiter names in emails), update the tracker entry's hiringManager field AND create/update HiringManagerOutreach.md in the application folder.
69. 
70. ### 11. Google Forms had stale draft values
71. Google Forms auto-saves drafts per Google account. Pre-filled values from previous incomplete sessions were incorrect (e.g., CTC showing 11.5 instead of 12).
72. **FIX**: Before submitting any Google Form discovered via email, verify ALL pre-filled values against candidate/current-status.json.
73. 
74. ### 12. Not updating data/manual-tasks.md
75. After finding new assessments, interviews, or action items in email, agents didn't add them to data/manual-tasks.md.
76. **FIX**: After processing each response, if it requires manual action (assessment, interview, ID verification), immediately add it to data/manual-tasks.md with link, tracker reference, and folder reference.
77. 
78. ### 13. Status changed but folder not moved
79. When marking an application as rejected/no-response, the tracker entry was updated but the folder was left in Applications/ instead of being moved to Applications/.archive/.
80. **FIX**: When closing an application, use `python scripts/archive_closed.py` or manually move the folder AND update the folderPath in the tracker entry.
```

</details>

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\check-responses.prompt.md.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\.github\prompts\check-responses.prompt.md (lines 80-180)

<details>
<summary>101 lines</summary>

````
80. **FIX**: When closing an application, use `python scripts/archive_closed.py` or manually move the folder AND update the folderPath in the tracker entry.
81. 
82. ---
83. 
84. ## THE RULES - NON-NEGOTIABLE
85. 
86. ### Rule 1: Correct Gmail account
87. ALL job email checking MUST use `mail.google.com/mail/u/1/` (t.ushara0901@gmail.com).
88. NEVER use `mail/u/0` (tushar.agarwal0901@gmail.com - personal).
89. Before EVERY Gmail action, visually verify the URL contains `/mail/u/1/`.
90. 
91. ### Rule 2: Read every email individually
92. Do NOT scan subject lines and move on. Open each email. Read the content. Many rejection emails have misleading subjects ("Update on your application" could be rejection or interview). Assessment links are often buried in email bodies.
93. 
94. ### Rule 3: Cross-reference with tracker
95. Every response you find MUST be matched to a tracker entry. Search by company name, role, or job ID in data/tracker-active.json. If the response is for a closed application, check data/tracker-closed.json.
96. 
97. ### Rule 4: Update tracker immediately
98. When you find a response:
99. - **Rejection:** Change status to "rejected", move entry from Active to Closed, move folder to .archive
100. - **Interview invite:** Change status to "interview", add to data/manual-tasks.md
101. - **Assessment link:** Change status to "assessment-completed" or "assignment", add to data/manual-tasks.md
102. - **Shortlisted/profile viewed:** Change status to "shortlisted", add note with date
103. - **Recruiter reply:** Change status to "replied", quote key info in notes
104. - **No response after 30 days:** Change status to "no-response", move to Closed
105. 
106. ### Rule 5: Update data/manual-tasks.md
107. Any item needing manual action (assessment, interview, ID verification, form completion) MUST be added to data/manual-tasks.md with:
108. - Status (assessment/in-progress/monitoring)
109. - What needs to be done
110. - Direct link to the platform
111. - Folder reference: `[folder](Applications/{Company}/{Role}_{ID}/)`
112. - Tracker reference: `Tracker: data/tracker-active.json -> {Month} -> "{company}"`
113. 
114. ### Rule 6: Validate JSON after every tracker edit
115. ```
116. python -c "import json; json.load(open('data/tracker-active.json', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('valid')"
117. python -c "import json; json.load(open('data/tracker-closed.json', encoding='utf-8-sig')); print('valid')"
118. ```
119. 
120. ### Rule 7: Export pages before taking action
121. Use `window.__UMD.extractFullPage()` to export any page that contains important new information (assessment details, interview scheduling, etc.) before clicking any buttons.
122. 
123. ### Rule 8: Set LinkedIn viewport on every new tab
124. Before navigating to any LinkedIn page:
125. ```
126. mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> viewport 1920x1080, tabId: <your tab>
127. ```
128. 
129. ### Rule 9: Post-session cleanup
130. After completing all checks:
131. 1. Run `python scripts/cleanup_formatting.py` to fix mojibake/formatting
132. 2. Validate all tracker JSONs
133. 3. Run `python scripts/check_stale.py` to identify stale applications
134. 4. Update data/manual-tasks.md - add new items, mark completed items as DONE
135. 5. Log any issues to `.temp/subagent-issues-log-{YYYYMMDD}.md`
136. 6. Reset viewport on any tabs modified: `mcp_cdp-browser_emulate -> clearViewport` or close the tabs
137.    - If user reports pages look zoomed/wrong: Ctrl+0 resets zoom, or close the tab
138. 
139. ### Rule 10: Human language
140. All notes, emails, and replies must use `-` not `-`, `->` not `→`, straight quotes. Short sentences. No AI-sounding phrases.
141. 
142. ---
143. 
144. ## CHECKING WORKFLOW - STEP BY STEP
145. 
146. ### Phase 0: Preparation
147. 
148. 1. Read `candidate/current-status.json` - note email, phone, current experience
149. 2. Read `data/tracker-active.json` - note all active applications, their statuses, dates
150. 3. Read `data/manual-tasks.md` - note what was previously flagged
151. 4. Group active applications by status for reference:
152.    - `applied` - waiting for any response
153.    - `in-progress` - incomplete forms, need action
154.    - `shortlisted` - company showed interest
155.    - `interview` - interview scheduled
156.    - `replied` - in conversation
157.    - `assignment` - take-home work
158.    - `assessment-completed` - waiting for results
159. 
160. ### Phase 1: Gmail Check (t.ushara0901@gmail.com)
161. 
162. **Setup:**
163. 1. Create a new browser tab via `mcp_cdp-browser_tabs` -> create
164. 2. Navigate to `https://mail.google.com/mail/u/1/#inbox`
165. 3. Wait for page to load fully
166. 4. VERIFY the URL contains `/mail/u/1/` - if it shows u/0, navigate again explicitly
167. 
168. **Check inbox:**
169. 1. Take a page snapshot to see all visible emails
170. 2. For EACH unread email (or recent email from last few days):
171.    a. Click on it to open it
172.    b. Read the FULL email content (use `window.__UMD.extractFullPage()` to capture it)
173.    c. Determine what kind of response it is:
174.       - **Rejection email** -> keywords: "unfortunately", "moved forward with other candidates", "not a fit", "position has been filled"
175.       - **Interview invite** -> keywords: "interview", "schedule a call", "next round", "meet the team"
176.       - **Assessment/test** -> keywords: "coding challenge", "assessment", "test link", "HackerRank", "HackerEarth", "complete by"
177.       - **Recruiter contact** -> keywords: "reviewed your profile", "interested in your background", "opportunity"
178.       - **OTP/verification** -> keywords: "verify", "OTP", "confirmation code"
179.       - **Application confirmation** -> keywords: "application received", "thank you for applying"
180.    d. Match the email to a tracker entry (search by company name, role title)
````

</details>

## ⚠️ Chrome Sessions Lost - Manual Sign-in Required

When I restarted Chrome with the CDP debug port (`--remote-debugging-port=9222`) to enable browser automation, **all login sessions were lost**. Google auth cookies are gone - Gmail, LinkedIn, and all other platforms now show sign-in pages.

### What happened
- Your Chrome was running WITHOUT `--remote-debugging-port=9222` (required for me to control the browser)
- I restarted it WITH the debug port using the same user data directory
- But Google session cookies didn't survive the restart (they were either session-only or DPAPI-encrypted in a way that reset)

### What you need to do

**Please sign in to these accounts in the Chrome window that just opened:**

1. **Google Account (personal first):** Go to `https://accounts.google.com` and sign in with `tushar.agarwal0901@gmail.com` (u/0)
2. **Google Account (job):** Add `t.ushara0901@gmail.com` as second account (u/1)
3. **LinkedIn:** Go to `https://www.linkedin.com` and sign in
4. **Hirist:** Go to `https://www.hirist.tech` and sign in

### Once you've signed in

Reply here and I'll immediately proceed with the full response-check workflow across all three platforms:
- **Gmail** - all unread + last 5 days, all labels including Dot 1, Spam, Promotions
- **LinkedIn** - messages, notifications, saved jobs
- **Hirist** - bell notifications

### For future sessions

To avoid this issue, always launch Chrome with the debug flag **before** starting a mail-check session:
```
"C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe" --remote-debugging-port=9222
```
Or add it to your Chrome shortcut so it's always enabled.
````

The snapshot is huge. Let me extract just the email list using JS.

<sub>⏱️ 28805m 22s</sub>

### ✅ `cdp-browser-execute`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "action": "eval",
  "tabId": "0416C7F3BC3F0DDD594FB669EFD1DED6",
  "expression": "\n(() => {\n  // Get all email rows from inbox\n  const rows = document.querySelectorAll('tr.zA');\n  const emails = [];\n  for (let i = 0; i < Math.min(rows.length, 30); i++) {\n    const row = rows[i];\n    const isUnread = row.classList.contains('zE');\n    const sender = row.querySelector('.yW .bA4 span, .yW span[email]')?.getAttribute('name') || row.querySelector('.yW')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    const subject = row.querySelector('.bog span, .y6 span')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    const snippet = row.querySelector('.y2')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    const date = row.querySelector('.xW span')?.getAttribute('title') || row.querySelector('.xW span')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    emails.push({ i: i+1, unread: isUnread, sender, subject, snippet, date });\n  }\n  return JSON.stringify(emails);\n})()\n"
}
```

</details>

### ✅ `cdp-browser-execute`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "action": "eval",
  "expression": "\n(() => {\n  // Search for rejection/interview/assessment keywords in recent emails\n  const rows = document.querySelectorAll('tr.zA');\n  const interesting = [];\n  const keywords = ['unfortunately', 'regret', 'not moving forward', 'interview', 'assessment', 'schedule', 'next round', 'shortlisted', 'selected', 'offer', 'hackerrank', 'hackerearth', 'codility', 'rejected', 'congratulations', 'nxtwave', 'OTP', 'verify', 'password'];\n  for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i++) {\n    const row = rows[i];\n    const text = row.textContent.toLowerCase();\n    const matchedKw = keywords.filter(kw => text.includes(kw.toLowerCase()));\n    if (matchedKw.length > 0) {\n      const sender = row.querySelector('.yW')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n      const subject = row.querySelector('.bog span, .y6 span')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n      const date = row.querySelector('.xW span')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n      interesting.push({ i: i+1, sender, subject, date, keywords: matchedKw });\n    }\n  }\n  return JSON.stringify(interesting);\n})()\n",
  "tabId": "0416C7F3BC3F0DDD594FB669EFD1DED6"
}
```

</details>

  "expression": "\n(() => {\n  const rows = document.querySelectorAll('tr.zA');\n  const emails = [];\n  for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i++) {\n    const row = rows[i];\n    const isUnread = row.classList.contains('zE');\n    const sender = row.querySelector('.yW')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    const subject = row.querySelector('.bog span, .y6 span')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    const snippet = row.querySelector('.y2')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    const date = row.querySelector('.xW span')?.getAttribute('title') || row.querySelector('.xW span')?.textContent?.trim() || '';\n    emails.push({ i: i+1, unread: isUnread, sender, subject: subject.substring(0, 100), snippet: snippet.substring(0, 150), date });\n  }\n  return JSON.stringify(emails);\n})()\n",
  "tabId": "0416C7F3BC3F0DDD594FB669EFD1DED6"
}
```

Hi Tushar,
 
Thank you so much for your interest in ClickUp!
 
The team was truly impressed by your background and the experiences you shared in your application. We appreciate you taking the time to apply and share your passion and skills with us.
 
We received a significant number of applications from qualified candidates like yourself, making this selection process extremely difficult. With that said, after careful review, the decision has been made to move forward with other candidates in the process at this time for the Analytics Engineer position.
 
While this specific role wasn't the perfect fit at this time, we want to encourage you to stay connected with ClickUp! Keep an eye on our careers page, as we're always growing and adding new roles.
 
We wish you all the best in your job search and we hope our paths will cross again soon. 
 
 
Best,
The ClickUp Team

### 💬 Copilot

Now I need to:
1. Move ClickUp, NeuroDrift, Hyqoo, ApplyAZ to closed tracker (rejected)
2. Update NxtWave to "shortlisted"

Let me do this efficiently with a script.

---

<sub>⏱️ 28810m 1s</sub>

### ✅ `powershell`

**Update tracker with rejections**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "python -c \"\nimport json\n\n# Read active tracker\nwith open('data/tracker-active.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:\n    active = json.load(f)\n\n# Read closed tracker\nwith open('data/tracker-closed.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:\n    closed = json.load(f)\n\n# Companies to reject\nreject_companies = {\n    ('ClickUp', 'Analytics Engineer'),\n    ('NeuroDrift', 'Voice AI Engineer'),\n    ('Hyqoo', 'AI Engineer'),\n    ('ApplyAZ', 'Full Stack Engineer'),\n}\n\n# Track entries to move\nmoved = []\nnxtwave_updated = False\n\nfor month_key in list(active.keys()):\n    if month_key == 'lastUpdated':\n        continue\n    entries = active[month_key]\n    remaining = []\n    for entry in entries:\n        company = entry.get('company', '')\n        role = entry.get('role', '')\n        key = (company, role)\n        \n        if key in reject_companies:\n            entry['status'] = 'rejected'\n            if key == ('ClickUp', 'Analytics Engineer'):\n                entry['notes'] = entry.get('notes', '') + ' | Rejection email May 6: moving forward with other candidates.'\n            elif key == ('NeuroDrift', 'Voice AI Engineer'):\n                entry['notes'] = entry.get('notes', '') + ' | LinkedIn rejection May 5: not moving forward.'\n            elif key == ('Hyqoo', 'AI Engineer'):\n                entry['notes'] = entry.get('notes', '') + ' | LinkedIn rejection May 5: not moving forward.'\n            elif key == ('ApplyAZ', 'Full Stack Engineer'):\n                entry['notes'] = entry.get('notes', '') + ' | LinkedIn rejection May 5: not moving forward.'\n            \n            # Add to closed under May-2026\n            if 'May-2026' not in closed:\n                closed['May-2026'] = []\n            closed['May-2026'].append(entry)\n            moved.append(f'{company} - {role}')\n        else:\n            # Update NxtWave to shortlisted\n            if company == 'NxtWave' and role == 'SDE II - Full Stack Technologies' and entry.get('jobId') == '1632499':\n                entry['status'] = 'shortlisted'\n                entry['notes'] = entry.get('notes', '') + ' | Hirist: Shortlisted + Downloaded May 8. Hiring Drive invite May 9 at WeWork Raheja Mindspace Hyderabad 9am-3pm.'\n                nxtwave_updated = True\n            remaining.append(entry)\n    active[month_key] = remaining\n\nactive['lastUpdated'] = '2026-05-09'\nclosed['lastUpdated'] = '2026-05-09'\n\nwith open('data/tracker-active.json', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n    json.dump(active, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n\nwith open('data/tracker-closed.json', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n    json.dump(closed, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n\nprint(f'Moved to closed: {moved}')\nprint(f'NxtWave updated: {nxtwave_updated}')\n\"\n",
  "description": "Update tracker with rejections"
}
```

</details>

The Software Engineer role at Trendlyne offers an exciting opportunity to work on innovative analytics products in the financial markets space. ### 💬 Copilot

The Cutshort links are tracking-redirect URLs. The easiest way is to reply to the email directly - Cutshort emails allow replying to express interest. }

}

<sub>⏱️ 28825m 57s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

This is the known issue - LinkedIn's Ember text area requires real `isTrusted` keystrokes per character, and CDP's `type` action injects text but Ember doesn't register it. The Send button stays disabled.

Let me try a different approach - use the Voyager Messaging API directly to send the message.

}

{
  "action": "eval",

I'm interested in the Software Engineering Expert role at Handshake AI. I have 2+ years of experience with Python, JavaScript, C#, and algorithms at MAQ Software. Happy to complete the assessment - could you share the application form link?\";\n  \n  // Send via Voyager messaging API\n  const resp = await fetch('https://www.linkedin.com/voyager/api/messaging/conversations/' + encodeURIComponent(mailboxUrn) + '/events', {\n    method: 'POST',\n    headers: {\n      'csrf-token': csrfToken,\n      'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n      'x-restli-protocol-version': '2.0.0'\n    },\n    credentials: 'include',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n      eventCreate: {\n        value: {\n          'com.linkedin.voyager.messaging.create.MessageCreate': {\n            body: msgText,\n            attachments: []\n          }\n        }\n      },\n      dedupeByClientGeneratedToken: false\n    })\n  });\n  \n  return JSON.stringify({ status: resp.status, ok: resp.ok });\n})()\n",
  "tabId": "4EA2D021FE67C6851AA99881C7782E93"
}
```

{
  "action": "eval",

I'm interested in the Software Engineering Expert role at Handshake AI. I have 2+ years of experience with Python, JavaScript, C#, and algorithms at MAQ Software. Happy to complete the assessment - could you share the application form link?\";\n  \n  // Try the Dashboard messaging API\n  const resp = await fetch('https://www.linkedin.com/voyager/api/voyagerMessagingDashMessengerMessages?action=createMessage', {\n    method: 'POST',\n    headers: {\n      'csrf-token': csrfToken,\n      'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n      'x-restli-protocol-version': '2.0.0'\n    },\n    credentials: 'include',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n      mailboxUrn: 'urn:li:fsd_profile:' + '', // we need the conversation entity URN\n      message: {\n        body: {\n          text: msgText\n        }\n      },\n      conversationUrn: 'urn:li:msg_conversation:(urn:li:fsd_profile:ACoAADOiE0IB_Gl8lkF2x6XYxATFMPiYJQKpWaE,' + threadId + ')'\n    })\n  });\n  \n  const text = await resp.text();\n  return JSON.stringify({ status: resp.status, body: text.substring(0, 300) });\n})()\n",
  "tabId": "4EA2D021FE67C6851AA99881C7782E93"
}

{
  "command": "python -c \"\nimport json\n\n# Add Trendlyne and Superclaims to tracker\nwith open('data/tracker-active.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8-sig') as f:\n    active = json.load(f)\n\nif 'May-2026' not in active:\n    active['May-2026'] = []\n\n# Check if already exists\nexisting = set()\nfor month in active:\n    if month == 'lastUpdated': continue\n    for e in active[month]:\n        existing.add((e.get('company',''), e.get('role','')))\n\nnew_entries = []\nif ('Trendlyne Technologies', 'Backend Engineer - Python/Django') not in existing:\n    new_entries.append({\n        'company': 'Trendlyne Technologies',\n        'role': 'Backend Engineer - Python/Django',\n        'platform': 'cutshort',\n        'listingUrl': 'https://cutshort.io',\n        'folderPath': 'Applications/Trendlyne/Backend Engineer Python Django',\n        'dateApplied': '2026-05-09',\n        'status': 'replied',\n        'notes': 'Cutshort invite from Maithri Umesh. Replied with interest May 9. Python/Django backend, 1-3yr exp, Series-A fintech analytics startup.'\n    })\n\nif ('Superclaims', 'Backend Python Developer') not in existing:\n    new_entries.append({\n        'company': 'Superclaims',\n        'role': 'Backend Python Developer',\n        'platform': 'cutshort',\n        'listingUrl': 'https://cutshort.io',\n        'folderPath': 'Applications/Superclaims/Backend Python Developer',\n        'dateApplied': '2026-05-09',\n        'status': 'replied',\n        'notes': 'Cutshort invite from Akshith Daithala. Replied with interest May 9. Python backend, 1-3yr exp, Hyderabad.'\n    })\n\nactive['May-2026'].extend(new_entries)\nactive['lastUpdated'] = '2026-05-09'\n\nwith open('data/tracker-active.json', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n    json.dump(active, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)\n\nprint(f'Added {len(new_entries)} new entries')\n\"\n",
  "description": "Add Trendlyne and Superclaims to tracker"
}

The form has 4 steps total:\n1. Personal Info (already done)\n2. Education and Experience (CURRENT - needs filling)\n3. Job Application Questions\n4. More About You\n\nUSE THIS TAB - do NOT open a new one.\n\n## Step 2: Education and Experience\n\n### Education\n- Degree: Bachelor of Technology - BTech (use the combobox dropdown, type \"BTech\" and select from list)\n- Major: Computer Science and Engineering\n- School: National Institute of Technology Delhi (type \"National Institute\" and select from dropdown)\n- Start Date: August 2019\n- End Date: May 2023\n- Graduated: Yes (check the checkbox)\nClick \"Add Education\" button after filling.\n\n### Experience\nClick \"Add Experience\" and fill:\n- Job Title: Software Engineer\n- Company: MAQ Software\n- Start Date: February 2024\n- End Date: Present / Currently Working\n- Description: Developed enterprise data platforms and ETL pipelines using C#, .NET, Python, Azure services. Built REST APIs, Power BI reports, and Cosmos DB solutions. Implemented CI/CD pipelines and automated testing.\n\n### Skills (add these one at a time via \"Add Skill\"):\nPython, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP, SQL, C#, .NET, REST APIs, Azure, Docker, Git, Power BI\n\n### Languages:\n- English: Fluent/Native\n- Hindi: Fluent/Native\n\n### Licenses/Certificates: Skip (none relevant)\n\nAfter filling Step 2, click \"Next\" to go to Step 3.\n\n## Step 3: Job Application Questions\nAnswer questions based on candidate info:\n- Years of experience: 2\n- Work authorization: Yes, authorized in India\n- Willing to relocate: Yes\n- Notice period: 30 days\n- Current CTC: 7.78 LPA (778000 INR per year)\n- Expected CTC: 12-14 LPA\n- Any questions about salary/relocation: answer honestly\n\n## Step 4: More About You\n- Gender: Male\n- Ethnicity: Asian\n- Veteran: No\n- Disability: No\n\n## Important\n- The combobox dropdowns need typing + selecting from the dropdown list that appears\n- For Degree: type \"Bachelor\" or \"BTech\", wait for dropdown, click the matching option\n- For School: type \"National Institute\", wait for dropdown, click the match\n- For date dropdowns: click the combobox, type the month/year, select from dropdown\n- DO NOT click Submit at the end - leave the form on the final review page for user to review\n\n## Candidate Info\n- Name: Tushar Agarwal\n- Email: t.ushara0901@gmail.com\n- Phone: +91 8670263451\n- DOB: 09/01/2002\n- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/tushar-agarwal-8aa137202/\n- GitHub: https://github.com/Tushar49\n\nReport what was filled in each step and any issues encountered.\n"
}

60.   function sanitizeText(text) {
61.     if (!text) return text;
62.     let t = text;
63.     t = t.replace(/\u2014/g, () => { bump('em-dash', 1); return '-'; });
64.     t = t.replace(/\u2013/g, () => { bump('en-dash', 1); return '-'; });
65.     t = t.replace(/[\u201C\u201D\u201E\u201F]/g, () => { bump('smart-double-quote', 1); return '"'; });
66.     t = t.replace(/[\u2018\u2019\u201A\u201B]/g, () => { bump('smart-single-quote', 1); return "'"; });
67.     t = t.replace(/\u2026/g, () => { bump('ellipsis', 1); return '...'; });
68.     t = t.replace(/[\u200B\u200C\u200D\u2060\uFEFF]/g, () => { bump('zero-width', 1); return ''; });
69.     t = t.replace(/\u00A0/g, () => { bump('nbsp', 1); return ' '; });
70.     return t;
71.   }
72. }

  "tabId": "8AC177F7FCB1207E363072D88B238725"
}

{
  "action": "eval",
  "expression": "\n(() => {\n  const articles = document.querySelectorAll('article');\n  const experiences = [];\n  for (const art of articles) {\n    const group = art.querySelector('fieldset, [role=\"group\"]');\n    if (group) {\n      const label = group.getAttribute('aria-label') || group.textContent.trim().substring(0, 80);\n      if (label.includes('Software') || label.includes('MAQ')) {\n        experiences.push(label);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return JSON.stringify(experiences);\n})()\n",
  "tabId": "8AC177F7FCB1207E363072D88B238725"
}

1. # CV - Tushar Agarwal

10. 
11. ## Professional Summary
12. 
13. Software Engineer with 2+ years at MAQ Software building production automation and enterprise tooling. Built a Python-Selenium automation system from scratch (3000+ LOC in 5 days) that cut 3-hour manual deployments to 1 hour - a problem the team had written off as not automatable. Designed the internal MCP server architecture that solved a 128-tool limit through nested routing, now used daily by the entire team. NIT Delhi CSE graduate, coding since 6th grade. Practical AI user who treats AI as a powerful assistant with guardrails, not an autonomous agent.
14. 
15. ---
16. 
17. ## Work Experience
18. 
19. ### MAQ Software - Hyderabad, India
20. 
21. **Software Engineer 1** | Jul 2024 - Present
22. 
23. - Built Python-Selenium pre-deployment automation for PowerApps flow solutions: handles 50+ connector types among 600+ possible, ~3000-4000 LOC, built solo in 5 days. Cut 3-hour manual deployment task to 1-hour automated run. Handles internet drops, session timeouts, UI loading states - runs entirely unattended
24. - Architected internal MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for AI assistant integration: solved 128-tool limit with nested architecture (10 top-level category tools x ~20 sub-tools each). Designed for team adoption across the engineering group
25. - Managed interns: coordinated work assignments, mentored on technical problems, ensured people stayed unblocked
26. - Technologies: Python, Selenium, PowerShell, JavaScript, C#/.NET, Dynamics CRM, PowerApps, Azure DevOps
27. 
28. **Software Engineer Intern** | Jan 2024 - Jul 2024
29. 
30. - Developed automation scripts for pre-deployment validation workflows

32. - Learned real production system patterns: messy, fragile, full of undocumented edge cases
33.

Skip to main content.
About Team Amex
 
Career Areas
 
Locations
 
Students
 
Jobs
American English
View More Jobs
AI Engineer II
Gurugram, HR, India
Bengaluru, KA, India
(Hybrid)
Apply Now
JOB DESCRIPTION

As part of our AI/ML Platform Engineering Tech team, you can architect, code and ship platform capabilities that power essential journeys that are part of our customers’ digital lives. Here, you can work alongside talented engineers in an open, supportive, inclusive environment where your voice is valued, and you make your own decisions on what tech to use to solve challenging problems. Amex offers a range of opportunities to work with the latest technologies and encourages you to back the broader engineering community through open source. And because we understand the importance of keeping your skills fresh and relevant, we give you dedicated time to invest in your professional development. Find your place in technology on #TeamAmex. 

RESPONSIBILITIES
Perform technical aspects of software development for assigned applications including design, developing prototypes, and coding assignments.
Perform hands-on architecture, design, and development of systems.
Drive consistent development practices with respect to tools, common components, and documentation.
Develop deep understanding of tie-ins with other Amex systems and platforms within the supported domains.
Perform ongoing refactoring of code, utilize visualization and other techniques to fast-track concepts, and deliver continuous improvement.
Identify opportunities to adopt innovative technologies.
Provide continuous support for ongoing application availability.
Enable business capabilities through innovation.
Research and learn new and upcoming technologies, and apply them to develop working software.
Debug software components and identify code defects for remediation.
Deploy, support, and monitor software across test, integration, and production environments.
Automatically scale applications based on demand projections.
Influence team members with innovative changes and improvements by challenging status quo and demonstrating risk taking.
Demonstrate increased self-reliance to achieve team goals.
QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum Qualifications

2+ years of software development experience with proven hands-on experience building data driven products.
Good in Computer Science fundamentals in object-oriented design, data structures, algorithm design, complexity analysis, problem solving and diagnosis
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Adept at any one of the programming language Java/Python and Open to learn new programming languages.
Well conversant with Linux / Unix tools
Critical Factors to Success (Outcome Driven)

 

Technical/Platforms:

Experience in large scale platform engineering

Experience in application development and integration within one or more Cloud platforms such as Google Cloud Platform, Amazon Web Services, etc.

Understanding of Machine Learning Ecosystem with hands on experience working on Airflow, Jupyterlab, Jupyterhub, MLFlow like frameworks. 
Experience with object-oriented design and coding with Python & related framework.
Proficiency on Linux.
Understanding and experience with UNIX / Shell scripting
Proficiency on distributed systems concepts like load balancing, shards, distributed transactions. 
Understanding of data structures, algorithms & Design Patterns

Experience building fast and scalable machine learning and analytical algorithms and executing all tasks from design to deployment and monitoring

Experience in developing Web services, micro-services, and REST

Experience working with distributed systems using Spark, Hive in Hadoop at significant scale in a production environment.

    

Good To Have:

Functional:

Familiarity with design & coding across multiple platforms & languages
Strong analytical & strategic thinking skills
Ability to interpret technical /business objectives and challenges
Porting/Software Configuration
Agile Methodologies
Software development/ testing
Product Engineering Knowledge
Platform engineering knowledge (e.g. Financial Tech/Ecommerce/ Aggregator platforms)

 

Business Outcomes:

Design and deliver innovative AIML / Big Data platform solutions across multiple business units
Identify and adopt new technologies to enhance user experience and predict future challenges

 

Leadership Outcomes:

Put enterprise thinking first, connect the role's agenda to enterprise priorities and balance the needs of customers, partners, colleagues & shareholders.
Lead with an external perspective, challenge status quo and bring continuous innovation to our existing offerings
Demonstrate learning agility, make decisions quickly and with the highest level of integrity

At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, shared values and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. From delivering differentiated products to providing world-class customer service, we operate with a strong risk mindset, ensuring we continue to uphold our brand promise of trust, security, and service.

As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.

We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:

Competitive base salaries
Bonus incentives
Support for financial-well-being and retirement
Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location)
Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need
Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location)
Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location)
Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program
Career development and training opportunities

American Express is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, disability status, age, or any other status protected by law.

Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at E:\Projects\JobApplications\templates\resume-style.css.**

E:\Projects\JobApplications\templates\resume-style.css

<details>
<summary>58 lines</summary>

```
1. /* Resume v8 - Matches Canva design */
2. :root {
3.   --primary-color: #5282b8;
4.   --text-dark: #2b2b2b;
5.   --text-gray: #555555;
6.   --line-color: #5282b8;
7.   --page-bg: #ffffff;
8. }
9. * { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
10. 
11. body { font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; background: #e9ecef; color: var(--text-dark); line-height: 1.5; padding: 40px 20px; display: flex; justify-content: center; }
12. 
13. .resume-page { width: 210mm; background: var(--page-bg); box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.15); display: flex; overflow: hidden; }
14. 
15. /* Left Column */
16. .left-column { width: 35%; background: var(--page-bg); padding: 28px 24px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; }
17. .header-left { margin-bottom: 20px; }
18. .name { font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; color: var(--primary-color); font-size: 24px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.1; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 1px; margin-bottom: 4px; }
19. .job-title { font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-gray); font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: .5px; }
20. .profile-pic-container { width: 100%; margin-bottom: 20px; }
21. .profile-pic { width: 100%; aspect-ratio: 1/1; object-fit: cover; background: #f0f0f0; display: block; }
22. .section-left { margin-bottom: 20px; }
23. .section-title-left { font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: var(--text-dark); font-weight: 600; text-transform: capitalize; border-top: 1.5px solid var(--primary-color); border-bottom: 1.5px solid var(--primary-color); padding: 5px 0; margin-bottom: 10px; }
24. .about-text { font-size: 10.5px; color: var(--text-gray); line-height: 1.5; text-align: justify; }
25. .contact-list { list-style: none; }
26. .contact-list li { display: flex; align-items: center; font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-gray); margin-bottom: 8px; word-break: break-word; }
27. .contact-icon { width: 20px; margin-right: 12px; color: var(--primary-color); display: flex; justify-content: center; }
28. .contact-list a { color: var(--text-gray); text-decoration: none; }
29. .contact-list a:hover { color: var(--primary-color); text-decoration: underline; }
30. .skills-list { list-style: disc; padding-left: 20px; }
31. .skills-list li { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-dark); font-weight: 400; margin-bottom: 8px; font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif; }
32. 
33. /* Right Column */
34. .right-column { width: 65%; padding: 28px 30px 28px 18px; }
35. .section-right { margin-bottom: 18px; }
36. .section-title-right { font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; font-size: 18px; color: var(--text-dark); font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 12px; text-transform: capitalize; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--primary-color); padding-bottom: 6px; }
37. .timeline { position: relative; padding-left: 20px; }
38. .timeline::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 5px; top: 5px; bottom: 0; width: 1.5px; background: var(--primary-color); }
39. .timeline-item { position: relative; margin-bottom: 14px; page-break-inside: avoid; }
40. .timeline-item:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }
41. .timeline-item::before { content: ''; position: absolute; left: -19.5px; top: 4px; width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--primary-color); border: 2px solid var(--page-bg); box-sizing: content-box; }
42. .item-header { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: flex-start; margin-bottom: 2px; }
43. .item-title { font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--text-dark); }
44. .item-date { font-size: 10.5px; color: var(--text-gray); white-space: nowrap; margin-left: 10px; margin-top: 2px; }
45. .item-subtitle { font-size: 11px; font-style: italic; color: var(--text-gray); margin-bottom: 3px; }
46. .item-desc { font-size: 10.5px; color: var(--text-gray); margin-bottom: 4px; }
47. .item-bullets { padding-left: 18px; margin-top: 5px; }
48. .item-bullets li { font-size: 10.5px; color: var(--text-gray); margin-bottom: 2px; line-height: 1.45; }
49. .tech-stack { font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif; font-size: 9px; color: var(--text-gray); font-style: italic; margin-top: -1px; margin-bottom: 3px; }
50. 
51. @media print {
52.   body { background: #fff; padding: 0; }
53.   .resume-page { box-shadow: none; min-height: 0; }
54.   * { -webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact; }
55.   .timeline::before { background: var(--primary-color) !important; }
56.   .timeline-item::before { background: var(--primary-color) !important; }
57. }
58.
```

```json
{
  "action": "eval",
  "expression": "\n(() => {\n  // Check all radio buttons - are they all answered?\n  const radioGroups = document.querySelectorAll('[role=\"radiogroup\"]');\n  const unanswered = [];\n  for (const rg of radioGroups) {\n    const label = rg.getAttribute('aria-label')?.substring(0, 60) || '';\n    const checked = rg.querySelector('input[type=\"radio\"]:checked, [role=\"radio\"][aria-checked=\"true\"], [role=\"radio\"][checked]');\n    if (!checked) unanswered.push(label);\n  }\n  // Also check for any textbox/textarea that's required and empty\n  const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('input[required]:not([type=\"radio\"]), textarea[required]');\n  const emptyRequired = [];\n  for (const inp of inputs) {\n    if (!inp.value?.trim()) emptyRequired.push(inp.getAttribute('aria-label') || 'unknown');\n  }\n  return JSON.stringify({ radioGroups: radioGroups.length, unanswered, emptyRequired });\n})()\n",
  "tabId": "EEEF3B4B8E0EB23FBF1BD27E38B828DE"
}
```

</details>

# AI Engineer III

> **Source:** https://careers.americanexpress.com/en/sites/CX_1/job/26007006/  
> **Captured:** 5/9/2026, 6:58:05 PM  
> **Description:** We are looking for engineers to build production-grade agentic AI systems that transform customer service experience across servicing channels. This role focuses on delivering measurable customer and operational outcomes through intelligent automation—not just building models.  

---

## Navigation

- [About Team Amex](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/about-teamamex/)
- [Career Areas](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/career-areas/)
- [Locations](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/locations/)
- [Students](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/student-programs/global-students-page.html)
- [Jobs](https://careers.americanexpress.com/en/sites/CX_1/jobs)
- [Deutsch]()

## Content

# AI Engineer III

 
   
  
   Bengaluru, KA, India   (Hybrid)   
 
   
 

 
      
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
  
  
  Apply Now    
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  

## Job Description

  


We are looking for engineers to build production-grade agentic AI systems that transform customer service experience across servicing channels. This role focuses on delivering measurable customer and operational outcomes through intelligent automation—not just building models.


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  

## Responsibilities

  

- Design, build, and deploy LLM-powered and agentic AI systems for real-time customer interactions across voice, chat, and messaging channels.
- Develop intelligent agents that:
  - Understand customer intent
  - Reason over context (interaction history, sentiment, policies)
  - Invoke enterprise tools (CRM, knowledge bases, ticketing systems)
  - Execute actions in real time and recover gracefully from failures
- Architect and implement scalable **RAG pipelines** over customer data, knowledge bases, and operational systems, ensuring:
  - High accuracy and low hallucination rates
  - Compliance and auditability
  - Strong data privacy and PII handling
- Build and extend shared AI platforms, including:
  - Conversational AI services
  - Agent orchestration frameworks
  - Real-time agent assist systems
  - Evaluation, monitoring, and observability tooling
- Own end-to-end system performance, including:
  - Reliability and fault tolerance
  - Low-latency response constraints (real-time systems)
  - Cost efficiency at scale
- Partner closely with product, operations, and CX design teams to deliver measurable outcomes such as:
  - Reduced resolution time
  - Increased containment
  - Improved CSAT
  - Enhanced agent productivity


**Technical Environment**


We operate in a modern, enterprise-scale environment focused on real-time, customer-facing AI systems. Strong fundamentals matter more than exact tool matching.


**Core Engineering Stack**


- **Languages:** Python, Go, TypeScript
- **Cloud & Infrastructure:** AWS and/or GCP, Kubernetes
- **APIs:** REST, gRPC
- **Distributed Systems:** Event-driven architectures (e.g., Kafka)
- **Datastores:** Postgres, MongoDB
- **Vector Databases:** Pinecone, Weaviate, FAISS
- **Async Processing:** Celery, Kafka
- **Deployment:** FastAPI, Docker, serverless
- **Observability:** LangSmith, Weights & Biases, Helicone


 


**Agentic AI & Machine Learning (CX Focus)**


- Hands-on experience integrating commercial and open-source LLMs into production workflows
- Experience building:
  - Agent orchestration systems for multi-step interaction handling
  - RAG pipelines over structured and unstructured enterprise data
  - Semantic search systems using vector databases
  - Evaluation frameworks (accuracy, hallucination, compliance, CX metrics)
- Strong practices in:
  - Conversation state management
  - Schema and tool interface design
  - Guardrails, validation, and safe execution in regulated environments


 


**AI-Assisted Development**


- Fluency with AI-assisted development workflows (code generation, testing, evaluation, iteration)
- Ability to use these tools effectively while maintaining production-grade engineering standards


 


**System Expectations**


All systems must meet enterprise standards for:


- Reliability in high-volume, real-time environments
- Security and data privacy (including PII handling)
- Auditability of automated decisions and interactions
- Responsible AI deployment in regulated customer contexts


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  

## Qualifications

  


**Required Qualifications**


- 5+ years of software engineering experience, including production systems involving LLMs, conversational AI, or applied ML
- Proven track record of building and deploying AI-powered systems in customer-facing or operational environments (e.g., chatbots, agent assist, automation)
- Strong engineering fundamentals across:
  - Backend systems
  - APIs
  - Data pipelines
  - Cloud infrastructure
- Hands-on experience with modern LLM tooling and agentic architectures
- Strong ownership mindset and ability to operate in ambiguous problem spaces
- Product-oriented thinking with focus on customer experience and operational impact


 


**Preferred Qualifications**


- Experience in regulated industries (financial services, healthcare, telecom)
- Experience in high-growth or transformation environments
- Track record of deploying systems that directly impact customer interactions at scale
- Contributions to open-source projects in AI, conversational systems, or developer tooling


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
     
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  


At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, [shared values](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/company/who-we-are/)and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. From delivering differentiated products to providing world-class customer service, we operate with a strong risk mindset, ensuring we continue to uphold our brand promise of trust, security, and service.

 

As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  


We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:

 


 
- Competitive base salaries
- Bonus incentives
- Support for financial-well-being and retirement
- Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location)
- Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need
- Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location)
- Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location)
- Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program
- Career development and training opportunities

 
 

American Express is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, disability status, age, or any other status protected by law.

 


 

Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.

> **Source:** https://careers.americanexpress.com/en/sites/CX_1/job/26006900/  
> **Captured:** 5/9/2026, 6:58:14 PM  
> **Description:** As part of our AI/ML Platform Engineering Tech team, you can architect, code and ship platform capabilities that power essential journeys that are part of our customers’ digital lives. Here, you can work alongside talented engineers in an open, supportive, inclusive environment where your voice is valued, and you make your own decisions on what tech to use to solve challenging problems. Amex offers a range of opportunities to work with the latest technologies and encourages you to back the broader engineering community through open source. And because we understand the importance of keeping your skills fresh and relevant, we give you dedicated time to invest in your professional development. Find your place in technology on #TeamAmex.  

---

## Navigation

- [About Team Amex](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/about-teamamex/)

## Content

# AI Engineer II

 
   
  
   Gurugram, HR, India
Bengaluru, KA, India
   (Hybrid)   
 
   
 

 
      
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
  
  
  Apply Now    
 
   
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  

## Job Description

  


As part of our AI/ML Platform Engineering Tech team, you can architect, code and ship platform capabilities that power essential journeys that are part of our customers’ digital lives. Here, you can work alongside talented engineers in an open, supportive, inclusive environment where your voice is valued, and you make your own decisions on what tech to use to solve challenging problems. Amex offers a range of opportunities to work with the latest technologies and encourages you to back the broader engineering community through open source. And because we understand the importance of keeping your skills fresh and relevant, we give you dedicated time to invest in your professional development. Find your place in technology on #TeamAmex. 


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  

## Responsibilities

  

- Perform technical aspects of software development for assigned applications including design, developing prototypes, and coding assignments.
- Perform hands-on architecture, design, and development of systems.
- Drive consistent development practices with respect to tools, common components, and documentation.
- Develop deep understanding of tie-ins with other Amex systems and platforms within the supported domains.
- Perform ongoing refactoring of code, utilize visualization and other techniques to fast-track concepts, and deliver continuous improvement.
- Identify opportunities to adopt innovative technologies.
- Provide continuous support for ongoing application availability.
- Enable business capabilities through innovation.
- Research and learn new and upcoming technologies, and apply them to develop working software.
- Debug software components and identify code defects for remediation.
- Deploy, support, and monitor software across test, integration, and production environments.
- Automatically scale applications based on demand projections.
- Influence team members with innovative changes and improvements by challenging status quo and demonstrating risk taking.
- Demonstrate increased self-reliance to achieve team goals.


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  

## Qualifications

  


**Minimum Qualifications**


- 2+ years of software development experience with proven hands-on experience building data driven products.
- Good in Computer Science fundamentals in object-oriented design, data structures, algorithm design, complexity analysis, problem solving and diagnosis
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
- Adept at any one of the programming language Java/Python and Open to learn new programming languages.
- Well conversant with Linux / Unix tools
- Critical Factors to Success (Outcome Driven)


 


**Technical/Platforms:**


- Experience in large scale platform engineering
- Experience in application development and integration within one or more Cloud platforms such as Google Cloud Platform, Amazon Web Services, etc.
- Understanding of Machine Learning Ecosystem with hands on experience working on Airflow, Jupyterlab, Jupyterhub, MLFlow like frameworks.
- Experience with object-oriented design and coding with Python & related framework.
- Proficiency on Linux.
- Understanding and experience with UNIX / Shell scripting
- Proficiency on distributed systems concepts like load balancing, shards, distributed transactions.
- Understanding of data structures, algorithms & Design Patterns
- Experience building fast and scalable machine learning and analytical algorithms and executing all tasks from design to deployment and monitoring
- Experience in developing Web services, micro-services, and REST
- Experience working with distributed systems using Spark, Hive in Hadoop at significant scale in a production environment.


 


**Good To Have:**


**Functional:**


- Familiarity with design & coding across multiple platforms & languages
- Strong analytical & strategic thinking skills
- Ability to interpret technical /business objectives and challenges
- Porting/Software Configuration
- Agile Methodologies
- Software development/ testing
- Product Engineering Knowledge
- Platform engineering knowledge (e.g. Financial Tech/Ecommerce/ Aggregator platforms)


 


**Business Outcomes:**


- Design and deliver innovative AIML / Big Data platform solutions across multiple business units
- Identify and adopt new technologies to enhance user experience and predict future challenges


 


**Leadership Outcomes:**


- Put enterprise thinking first, connect the role's agenda to enterprise priorities and balance the needs of customers, partners, colleagues & shareholders.
- Lead with an external perspective, challenge status quo and bring continuous innovation to our existing offerings
- Demonstrate learning agility, make decisions quickly and with the highest level of integrity


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
     
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  


At American Express, our culture is built on a 175-year history of innovation, [shared values](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/company/who-we-are/)and Leadership Behaviors, and an unwavering commitment to back our customers, communities, and colleagues. From delivering differentiated products to providing world-class customer service, we operate with a strong risk mindset, ensuring we continue to uphold our brand promise of trust, security, and service.

 

As part of Team Amex, you’ll experience our powerful backing with comprehensive support for your holistic well-being and many opportunities to learn new skills, develop as a leader, and grow your career. Here, your voice and ideas matter, your work makes an impact, and together, you will help us define the future of American Express.


 
 
    
 
   
 
   
 
  
     
   
  
  


We back you with benefits that support your holistic well-being so you can be and deliver your best. This means caring for you and your loved ones' physical, financial, and mental health, as well as providing the flexibility you need to thrive personally and professionally:

 


 
- Competitive base salaries
- Bonus incentives
- Support for financial-well-being and retirement
- Comprehensive medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability benefits (depending on location)
- Flexible working model with hybrid, onsite or virtual arrangements depending on role and business need
- Generous paid parental leave policies (depending on your location)
- Free access to global on-site wellness centers staffed with nurses and doctors (depending on location)
- Free and confidential counseling support through our Healthy Minds program
- Career development and training opportunities

 
 

American Express is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran status, disability status, age, or any other status protected by law.

 


 

Offer of employment with American Express is conditioned upon the successful completion of a background verification check, subject to applicable laws and regulations.

# Software Engineer II

> **Source:** https://careers.americanexpress.com/en/sites/CX_1/job/26006349/?utm_source=external%20referral&utm_medium=referral  
> **Captured:** 5/9/2026, 6:59:59 PM  
> **Description:** Platform Services is part of Global Infrastructure, responsible for Private & Public Cloud, Databases , CI/CD and Enterprise Observability. Database Platform is a group in Platform Services, which provides Architecture, Engineering, and support of DBMS software and infrastructure to ensure we offer high availability/recovery/performance solutions to the Development teams and Content DBAs. We are driving the adoption of self-service capabilities through APIs for health checks, backups, restarts, and administrative operations, empowering users and reducing operational dependency. By integrating AI-driven automation, observability, and intelligent tooling, we continuously enhance platform capabilities to improve efficiency, reduce MTTR, and enable proactive operations.  

---

## Navigation

- [About Team Amex](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/about-teamamex/)
- [Career Areas](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/career-areas/)
- [Locations](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/locations/)
- [Students](https://www.americanexpress.com/en-us/careers/student-programs/global-students-page.html)
